programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan suspects a TV hostess of murdering a controversial radio presenter - but she seems to have the perfect alibi. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke, Morton Downey Jr and Vicki Lawrence

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A loan shark, a stockbroker and a group of women pose problems for Dr Sloan as he tries to track down the person responsible for a friend's murder. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble flees when a burglary at his workplace leads to a check of employee fingerprints, but finds himself stuck on a bus caught in a road washout.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A woman asks for help after she and her boyfriend are constantly harassed by a jealous suitor, but after the stalker is found dead, Perry must prove she didn't do it.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A Marine Corps officer vows to avenge his sister's murder, and Walker must find him before another life is lost. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a hotel frequented by foreign diplomats after a woman's body is found wrapped in bedsheets. They tangle with a trade representative whose murky past suggests he could be the killer. Meanwhile, Danny argues with his boss over the corpse of a homeless man found covered with silver paint. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Two FBI agents try to seize control of the team's investigation into the discovery of a perfectly preserved body hidden in a chimney at a marine base. Reluctant to be ousted from the case, Gibbs agrees to work with them - and learns the victim was a serial killer responsible for the deaths of 14 people. Meanwhile, McGee's literary efforts lead to discord with the others

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team tries to find the culprit behind an ambush on a vehicle transporting marines in the desert, which resulted in the death of one man and abduction of two others. Kensi and Deeks bond as they track the SUV that took the missing soldiers, and Sam risks using a compromised cover identity to meet a former contact in a bid to discover if drug cartels were involved. Meanwhile, Nate returns from his mystery assignment

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble becomes a sitting duck when he is trapped underground after an explosion in a shaft and Gerard, alerted by a picture in the paper, arrives on the scene.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A pregnant legal secretary is mugged, and the assault causes her to loses her baby. The subsequent investigation pinpoints her lover as the prime suspect, and he is faced with a charge of foetal murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker's ex-con friend goes undercover to bust open a gang of criminals. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. The team discovers the cardiac arrest was caused by someone hacking into a computer monitoring Wiley's pacemaker and increasing his heart rate to 400bpm. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine with top-level security clearance, whose body has been discovered in a Beverly Hills car park - minus a hand. As inquiries proceed, the case proves to have unexpected ramifications, including the theft of jewels from Iraqi museums

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo investigates a murder at the military academy he once attended, after a murdered marine and alumni of the school is found clutching a photo of a current student. Meanwhile, Leo examines the staged death of a conceptual artist

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Retired DI Frank Patterson presents the team with information regarding the getaway car used in a 30-year-old safety deposit-box robbery. They discover the former detective not only wants to convict the suspected thief of the heist and murder of his three colleagues, but also prove that a high-ranking officer was involved in the plot - jeopardising the future of Sandra's Ucos unit. Phil Daniels guest stars

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Tom and his team set out to retrieve disgraced former MP Hampton Wilder's memoirs, which contain information capable of provoking a scandal big enough to bring down the government. Unfortunately, details are leaked to the press, leaving MI5 to question who is really pulling the strings. With guest stars Tim Pigott-Smith, Nicholas Farrell and David Calder

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A teenage girl is found tortured to death at a London dogs' home, and it soon emerges another woman is being held by the culprits, who turn out to be a violent team of animal activists seeking to destroy an experimentation facility. However, the case becomes much more complicated when Nikki is kidnapped at gunpoint by the group leader

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports live from a specialist police rehab unit in Berkshire, and Rav Wilding finds out about the lengths farmers will go to protect animals

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines a lower ground flat in Pimlico, central London. Martel Maxwell is in Ipswich, Suffolk, and Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, visiting properties bought at auction and both in need of renovation

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A Merseyside drug dealer has his BMW seized and sold off at auction. Meanwhile, police in Derby are after a man they suspect is part of a 'county lines' drug operation

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Liverpool City Council's housing team investigate a rental property that is so dangerous for its residents it has to be immediately shut down

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: After three months, Annie is still struggling to come to terms with Harry's death, Nancy and Lawrence embark on a full-blown affair and Joyce faces a dilemma about her husband. Esther fears Nancy may be a Nazi collaborator and food rations officer Ernest Luckhurst arrives at Finch's farm intent on catching him out

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history of the Vegetarian Society and discover how the movement changed public attitudes and became part of the mainstream. They also consider how British gardening has influenced the nation's cuisine, and provide a few recipes along the way

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life in the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and a couple of gold plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Life at Colchester Zoo for curator Sarah Forsyth, who heads into the busiest summer of her life keeping her animals happy while four out of five of her staff are furloughed. The documentary follows Sarah for two weeks, entirely inside the walls of one of Britain's largest zoos, as she works tirelessly to deal with the summer baby boom, animal emergencies and unique situations created by the absence of paying public

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock. Silversmith Brenton West is on hand to fix a precious metal box that is tarnished, tattered and coming apart at the seams. Made of a delicate metal called antimony, it poses a real challenge for Brenton, who isn't helped when Steve warns him of his own youthful experience trying to repair antimony

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Just six weeks after his wedding, a 28-year-old man named Darren disappears, leaving Inspector Simon Orton and the team at Durham Police baffled. As the detectives dig deeper into the events leading up to his disappearance, they uncover a series of secrets and lies, and begin to realise all is not what it seems. Clare McElvaney and her partner from South Durham CID have to work out which information they have been given is relevant to their case, and which is not

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels off-base for the high society wedding of Abigail's cousin. Raelle throws herself into her relationship with Scylla, who, unbeknownst to Raelle, faces an impossible choice. Abigail becomes disillusioned with her privilege while amongst the notable Bellweather line. Tally gets hit with two life-altering revelations that change the very fabric of the unit

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail has to come to terms with her vulnerability in the aftermath of a horrific event. Tally struggles with being supportive, while also keeping a secret from her unit. Raelle takes extreme measures to connect with Scylla

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Shropshire, meeting officers who reveal how they are taking knives off the streets. Rav Wilding finds out what counter-terrorism experts advise in a crisis

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts looks round a one-bedroom flat in Tunbridge Wells and Martel Maxwell examines the state of a former supermarket building in Derbyshire and a mid-terrace in Liverpool

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Following a modern slavery team as they check for signs of workers being exploited. Plus, how an American Football team in the UK received a boost from proceeds of crime funding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright visits a rented terraced house that is overcrowded and riddled with cockroaches. He then visits North Ayrshire, where the council is cracking down on empty homes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham to take to auction in Sheffield. Anita also brings along a mystery object that has people scratching their heads

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Finch hires families displaced by the bombing as cheap labour, but when a break-in at the Manor House leads Lady Hoxley to order them out, the situation escalates into a riot. Bea does not have time to answer Billy's proposal as she goes into labour, and Joyce reveals the truth about the affair when the Hoxleys host a party

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the food provided by British waterways as they fly-fish for trout, prepare a meal of stuffed wild duck and discover how the Gloucestershire tradition of eating baby eels has virtually disappeared

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a married couple with a £500,000 budget find a romantic retirement home in the Dorset countryside. Nicki also meets members of the public committed to refurbishing the region's distinctive 'finger posts'

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson try to bring an Alpine-cum-Oktoberfest element to a couple's garden in Chesham, Buckinghamshire, to give Munich-born wife Lisa a taste of home

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture experts, horologists, metal workers, ceramicists and upholsterers restore more family heirlooms - featuring an art deco clock, a teddy bear and a 3D photograph viewer

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace consider some of the very best MasterChef highlights, from the most amazing dishes to outstanding challenges to some of the most impressive individual success stories. John and Gregg also reflect on what it takes to be 'the best', and how finalists and winners start to shine during the culinary rounds - in particular when in 2012 the semi-finalists took over the brasserie on a cross-channel ferry. Then the judges revisit one of the final challenges from 2008, when three celebrities faced cooking with Marcus Wareing - which resulted in one of the most memorable moments in Celebrity MasterChef history

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The second of a two-part story picks up where things left off last week, with Humphrey, Florence and Dwayne having swapped the sun and sand of the Caribbean for the cold and noise of London. Their latest investigation has taken them back to the detective inspector's home town - but they've hit a brick wall with the discovery of another dead body. And now they have two murders to solve. Meanwhile, Dwayne has an emotional encounter and Humphrey receives advice from an unexpected source that helps him reach a big decision. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall, Danny John-Jules and Josephine Jobert, with guest appearances from Ardal O'Hanlon and Sally Bretton

programme.title: I Am Not a Rapist
programme.desc: Of the 60,000 rape cases reported to police last year, it is estimated that up to eight per cent were false allegations. This documentary hears the stories of three young men who were falsely accused of rape, examining the consequences of the allegations on their lives and the lives of their loved ones

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Wales to take a behind-the-scenes look at the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, revealing how its crew members keep people safe on the seas. Plus, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a fairly modern two-bedroom flat in Manchester, there is a revisit to a property in Bexleyheath, Kent, that was first seen in 2012, and Dion Dublin is in Wolverhampton examining a semi-detached house

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Cameras follow drug raids across Lancashire and beyond. Plus, an impressive racing car is put up for sale at the proceeds-of-crime auction and the tale of how a woman helped police get the better of a romance fraudster in Durham is recounted. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the scandal of a north London bedsit that's simply not fit for purpose. He also joins the Birmingham empty homes officer who's fighting back against the city's 10,000 empty properties

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Nancy is shocked when Ellen reveals Lawrence's dark secret. Annie is forced to choose between her duty to Harry and her love for Adam, Bea discovers Finch has doubts about her motives for marrying Billy, and Tucker's murderous revenge has devastating consequences for all four of the women. Drama, starring Summer Strallen and Nathaniel Parker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explain how necessity often forced cooks to make the most of limited ingredients to create tasty dishes. They reveal that many popular meals were invented during hard times, from mince and dumplings to Scotch broths

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Cornwall on a property search with a couple who have £750,000 to realise their dream of starting their married life together near the Cornish coast. While in the area, Nicki visits Looe Harbour to learn about the importance of sustainable fishing methods, which is championed by the local fishmongers she meets

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builder's rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention, spurring restorer Will Kirk into action. Also being given a much-needed makeover are an air-powered toy car and a RNLI donation box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Rich brothers Harry and David head to Corby, Northamptonshire to meet Keri and Mark. The pair don't get to share too much time together, so though they love the pub, instead of going out they want to create a pub garden at home. Charlie and the siblings assess the pair's boring and featureless plot and come up with ideas as to how it can be turned into a relaxing and tranquil social space for Keri, Mark and their friends. With a whole garden to make over and a proper bar to incorporate, the challenge promises to be a tough task to pull off

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The Browns await the fruits of their fraud after faking Grandad's death to get a payout, but the insurance company starts to grow suspicious. Agnes struggles to keep him out of sight, as she has promised the money to Dermot and Maria, who are desperate to get a place of their own

programme.title: Zodiac (2007)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime thriller about the tortuous hunt for a mysterious serial killer who terrorised San Francisco in the late 1960s and early 1970s, and taunted the police and media with cryptic messages. Three men became obsessed with identifying the murderer after his reign of terror inexplicably stopped, but their fixation with the case took a heavy toll on their lives. Starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Robert Downey Jr, Mark Ruffalo and Brian Cox

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help the Rev Karen and husband Colin, who have £6,000 to turn their oddly-shaped and weed-filled garden in Romsey into an Italian retreat

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a bus conductor's ticket machine, a Victorian automaton that can play the piano, and a former world champion's kickboxing trophy

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms in Scotland and the Lake District. In Armadale, a hill farmer prepares for one of Europe's biggest sheep sales, while in Haweswater, a couple take on the biggest gamble of their lives by moving to a larger farm 10 miles north. An Aberdeenshire couple who began farming four years ago plan to raise baby ostriches, and a Northumberland shepherd prepares for the National English Sheepdog trials, and new farmers hatch ostriches

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin returns with the antiques-buying challenge in which people are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown. In the first edition, hairdresser Sarah faces off against civil servant Marika in Liverpool

programme.title: The Cruel Sea (1953)
programme.desc: The veteran commander of a British escort vessel guides his inexperienced crew through the dangers of combat at sea during the Second World War Atlantic campaigns. Demoralised by the failure of his last command, the captain faces a life-or-death decision in his duty to sink an enemy vessel. Nautical action adventure, starring Jack Hawkins, Donald Sinden, Stanley Baker and Denholm Elliott

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Myleene Klass takes an aerial journey over Norwich to explore the effect the Blitz had on her home city from the perspective of those that dropped the bombs

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A compilation edition, featuring finds at Bletchley Park, the Ulster Transport Museum and Crowcombe Court. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers a fascinating family history in Cornwall

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Cornish poultry farmer Rebecca Tonks hopes to attract attention to her new egg products at the Speciality Fine Food Fair at London's Olympia. Northampton-based Tom and Tina Warner aim to impress potential buyers with their selection of farm produced gins. Narrated by Bill Paterson

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Former cricketer David Gower and comedian and TV presenter Nick Hancock tour Hampshire for antiques and collectibles that will raise them money at auction in Sidcup, with expert help from Charlie Ross and Philip Serrell. Along the way, David and Charlie board HMS Warrior, and Nick and Phil go hunting for brown trout

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the opening episode, Mary heads back to the city where she learnt to cook, Paris, where she visits one of the city's creperies to finesse her technique. Later, inspired by pastries in Parisian patisserie windows, she makes her own brioche and apple frangipane tart

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the first episode, Nadiya kicks things off with a series of her favourite recipes for classic bakes with a twist, including a blueberry scone pizza, and a spicy Asian take on a traditional toad-in-the-hole

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Lydia Quigley is forced to move her girls to the establishment of her old ally, Mrs May. Meanwhile, Charlotte is playing a dangerous game with the Fitzwilliams. Period drama set in London in 1763 following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Blayne enjoys a very expensive night with Charlotte, but is shocked to find out that Isabella has invited both her and Mrs Quigley to his card party. Meanwhile, Margaret is determined to get vengeance on Lydia, and persuades Justice Hunt to take action. Starring Julian Rhind-Tutt and Jessica Brown Findlay

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: A new redevelopment scheme is launched on the edge of the city centre, intended to create nearly 15,000 new properties, but leaving many residents afraid their homes will be demolished to make way for tower blocks. Among those at risk is a woman who is caring for her elderly bed-bound father, who urgently needs to know how long they can stay in the family home. At the West Tower, interior designer Sarah works on a penthouse for a millionaire client, spending thousands of pounds on bespoke furniture

programme.title: The Trial of Alex Salmond
programme.desc: In March this year, Scotland's former First Minister Alex Salmond emerged from the High Court in Edinburgh cleared of 14 charges of sexual misconduct, with the verdict concluding one of the most dramatic trials Britain has seen. This programme sees Kirsty Wark travelling across Scotland as she follows the trial and interviews people who were present at key moments surrounding the political scandal, and looking back at Salmond's political career and the growing rift between him and Nicola Sturgeon

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A stone carver, a fine art painter and a heraldic artist pitch ideas for a piece of art to celebrate the 850th anniversary of a Scottish castle. Hosted by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the murder of a military veteran by his estranged wife in 1825

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the case of a woman executed for killing her husband in 1856, which inspired Thomas Hardy's Tess of the d'Urbervilles

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kira in Slough

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and a couple of gold plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On a railway journey from the capital Palermo, through the ancient town of Agrigento and the port of Siracusa, to Europe's largest volcano, Mount Etna, Michael Portillo explores Sicilian life under the dictatorship. He also finds out how the dictator took on the Mafia and asks whether it is true that under Mussolini, the trains ran on time

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Antiques-buying challenge presented by Paul Martin, in which a marketing expert and a saleswoman in Stafford are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny (1954)
programme.desc: US Navy officers rebel against their ship's erratic captain during a violent storm at sea, only to face a court martial back on dry land. However, the crew's actions seem to have been justified when evidence questioning the mental stability of their superior is produced. Second World War drama based on Herman Wouk's novel, starring Humphrey Bogart, Jose Ferrer, Fred MacMurray and Van Johnson

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Broadcaster John Humphrys explores the effect the Blitz had on his home city of Cardiff, retracing the Luftwaffe's bombing routes over the Welsh capital, as well as Swansea

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In the great nave of Norwich Cathedral, experts Kate Bateman and David Fletcher pick out some chose items to take to auction. Among them are an enamel topped silver trinket box and a vintage model car. Plus, presenter Paul Martin uncovers the meaning of religious graffiti

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Natalie Davies from Worcester and Cate Morris from Gloucestershire use flowers from their farms to create floral displays to impress the judges at the Malvern Autumn Show

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paralympians and gold medal winners Danny Crates and Liz Johnson shop their way around Suffolk and Essex buying up items to take to auction in Huntingdon, Cambridgeshire. Experts David Harper and Arusha Irvine are on hand to help

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thousand-mile journey from the Swedish capital of Stockholm to Abisko in the northern reaches of the Arctic Circle. In Stockholm, Michael braves a precarious tour of the city from its rooftops before boarding a heritage tram to get the lowdown on 1930s Sweden from an expert. Moving on, he finishes his journey deep in the Arctic Circle at a remote climate research station. He also has lunch with traditional Sami people in Vilhelmina and checks into an ice hotel

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Classic routines from the Hammersmith Apollo on the subject of 'Transport'. Featuring rib-tickling routines from the likes of Michael McIntyre, Gina Yashere, Lenny Henry, Rachel Parris, Chris McCausland, Jason Manford, Ardal O'Hanlon and Josh Widdicombe

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: Willie has been in hospital a few days after his collapse, and although he seems to be doing well, the doctor informs the family he needs a new kidney - and the first port of call is always the children. All it takes is a simple blood test - but for Stuart this means facing up to his lifelong fear of needles. Meanwhile, a drunken Ted, having been thrown out by Kate, is desperate for her to take him back, and the result of Bertha's DNA test is due at any moment - with April's smug ex-fiance Humphrey refusing to go anywhere until he hears where he stands

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tries to help make sense of bewildering times as he dissects the week's news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction. Joining him this evening are Sara Pascoe, Sophie Duker and Jamali Maddix

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An overweight man turns up at a boxing gym and asks to be turned into a killing machine, much to the exasperation of the trainer. Short buddy comedy, written by and starring Nick Helm, with Lloyd Everitt and Jonathan Pointing

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Norfolk, where they attempt to ensnare a large pike - a fish that has eluded them on their trips so far. As they arrive and get their gear together, Bob introduces Paul to the surprisingly varied contents of his car boot, much to Paul's amusement. They gather their equipment and find a decent spot that should give them plenty of chances to catch and make camp for the day. They end their trip with a visit to a local pub, where Bob reveals a hidden trick

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at the art of making a historical documentary. He examines a wide range of shows, including Kenneth Clarke's Civilisation, the works of AJP Taylor, James Burke and Mary Beard, all the way to Who Do You Think You Are and Danny Dyer's Right Royal Family, to discover what it takes to make a great TV history lesson

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Local property developer Tim hits a million-pound problem in the mill he's converting into luxury apartments, while less than a mile away Anne and her friends face losing their homes to a giant redevelopment scheme. A homelessness support charity Lifeshare is forced to leave the Victorian Charter Street Mission, which it has operated out of for more than 30 years, due to redevelopment of the surrounding area. However, new businesses are also relocating to the city, which leads to two Glasgow IT workers looking for accommodation

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists head to Chester, where the buyer wants something to mark her 10th year of singing in an award-winning choir. With £500 on offer, will it be a glass maker who works on a barge, a collage artist who re-works magazines, or a textile worker of wool who makes the buyer's day? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein explore the drowning of a wife and mother in Victorian Bath, re-evaluating the chain of evidence which led to the conviction of her husband, Harry Dainton, for her murder

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson try to bring an Alpine-cum-Oktoberfest element to a couple's garden in Chesham, Buckinghamshire, to give Munich-born wife Lisa a taste of home

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps an aspiring writer from London to find a new home in Dumfries and Galloway, and also heads for the hills on a Scottish gold rush

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea welcomes 93-year-old Dora Paul to the workshop, along with the unusual item she has brought in for repair - an old tram seat which once ferried passengers on the number 81 tram in Salford, Greater Manchester. Jay Blades and the team also tackle a vintage horse-racing game and two stained-glass panels

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Norfolk, IT man Dominic faces off against train driver Martin. Paul Martin teaches them the tricks of the trade and turns them into successful antiques dealers

programme.title: In Which We Serve (1942)
programme.desc: When a British destroyer is torpedoed during the Battle of Crete, surviving crew members are left clinging to the wreckage and recall their previous adventures at sea - and the reasons why they enlisted for service in the first place. Second World War drama, starring Noel Coward, who co-directed with David Lean, alongside John Mills, Bernard Miles, Celia Johnson and Richard Attenborough

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Birmingham's factories were crucial to war production, and though the city was heavily bombed during the Second World War, much of the destruction was kept secret. Hollywood actor David Harewood takes an aerial journey over his home city to see the land from the perspective of the German pilots and talks to survivors on the ground about their recollections of the attacks

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The elephant and mole rat start their lives looking ancient and remain that way into old age, yet they outlive other animals their size. The veteran broadcaster considers the virtues of both creatures

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts touch down at Bletchley Park, the Ulster Transport Museum and Crowcombe Court to track down some unusual items to sell at auction. They uncover a beautiful watercolour and some stunning porcelain. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits Antony in Cornwall to unearth a fascinating family history

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radio host Dev Griffin, comedian Alex Horne, presenter June Sarpong and the lovely Debbie McGee go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: At the Moreton-in-Marsh Show in the Cotswolds, Betty Judge shows 21 of her pygmy goats, while Laura Ketley exhibits her best dairy Toggenburg goats

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors Bill Simons and Derek Fowlds hunt for antiques in Helmsley, North Yorkshire, before concluding with an auction in Ilkley, West Yorkshire. Bill's keen eye for unusual items secures him some unexpected lots, while Derek charms his way to great discounts. But will the antique hunt grind to a halt when the classic car breaks down?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reaps harvest from his vegetable garden, plants up a seasonal pot and gives a masterclass on how to make the perfect compost. Adam Frost travels to York to meet a couple who took on 20 acres of land and filled it with flowers, and in Walsall cameras visit a garden planted with exotic palms and foliage that has made a positive contribution to the owner's mental health. Plus, another chance to see a collection of late summer flowering bergamot plants in Wales and the zoologist in Lancashire who has spent his life exploring the microscopic insects that live in the soil

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian explores the enduring and powerful legacy the Romantics have left on the modern world. In this episode, Simon explores how Romantic artists created the secular icons of the modern democratic age, whose power continues to inspire the passions of revolt to this day. He considers the elixir of rebellion and the idea - so powerful in the words and images of William Blake - that imaginative passion can conquer mechanical logic and can create an art for the people. With contributions from actors Harriet Walter and Christopher Eccleston, hip-hop artist Testament and the French street artist PBoy

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert joins a crew of road workers in south Wales to find out what maintaining the highways and byways is really like. All the signs point to appalling weather, a risky environment and back-breaking labour, but if he can get the team to 'dig' his digging, can road-work-rookie Gilbert bag himself the best seat on the job?

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Unsure until they share their responses, Tom and Louise discuss the rekindling of their relationship. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the opening match of the three-game series, which came from Emirates Old Trafford

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: First episode of the sci-fi drama reinventing the cult 1970s series, starring Edward James Olmos as starship commander William Adama, charged with leading an attempt to find Earth. When Galactica is contacted by the Olympic Carrier, a vessel with more than 1,300 crew, Cdr Adama is advised there is a traitor on board. With Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Boomer wakes up soaking wet, covered in red stains and carrying an explosive - and six more bombs are missing from the locker. Unable to remember where she has been or what she has been doing, she believes that her body is being controlled by some kind of exterior force, possibly intending her to sabotage Galactica's water supply against her will

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: The Unbelievable Story of Carl Beech
programme.desc: Vanessa Engle's documentary telling the story of the man who claimed he had been sexually abused by a group of prominent men in the 1970s and '80s, naming Jimmy Savile, ex-prime minister Edward Heath and former home secretary Leon Brittan as among those involved. The film is constructed using Beech's own words, taken from media and police interviews and from his blogs, and there are interviews with those who were close to the story at the time, including his ex-wife and Diana Clemetson, the widow of Lord Brittan

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: A hot summer swimming spot is the latest painting to take shape under the brush of American artist Bob Ross

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit archaeological digs across the country, beginning with a look at the work being carried out in the west of Britain. The presenters visit Salisbury Museum, finding out about developments at sites including the enormous lost Welsh city of Trellech and Kent's Cavern, a prehistoric cave where Britain's earliest human remains have been found

programme.title: Jumbo: The Plane That Changed the World
programme.desc: Documentary examining the origins and development of the Boeing 747, which was the world's first wide-bodied commercial airliner and revolutionised air travel after coming into service in 1970. In interviews filmed on the original prototype, chief engineer Joe Sutter and his team describe the challenges they faced, from exploding engines to vibrations that threatened to tear the aircraft apart, and test pilot Brien Wygle reveals how on the first flight they hooked up car batteries as back-ups in case of engine failure

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary following workers at Rolls-Royce's Derby factory as they construct the company's Trent 1000 jet engine in preparation for its maiden flight attached to a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. The film explores the technology involved in its construction, and charts the ups and downs of working on the assembly line. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher

programme.title: Africa Turns the Page: The Novels That Shaped a Continent
programme.desc: Nigerian-born presenter and historian David Olusoga explores the story of the African novel, and a time when writers including Chinua Achebe, Ngugi wa Thiong'o and Wole Soyinka became the conscience of a continent. In their wake, the African novel was to spread around the world - writers of the African diaspora such as Buchi Emecheta and Ben Okri created masterpieces from their adopted home of the United Kingdom. Featuring contributions by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie, Aminatta Forna and 2019 Booker winner Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines how Denmark emerged from modest beginnings to become one of the greatest powers and arbiters of taste in northern Europe. He tells a story of incredible transformation befitting the homeland of Hans Christian Andersen, creator of The Ugly Duckling and The Emperor's New Clothes among many other fairy tales

programme.title: Carved with Love: The Genius of British Woodwork
programme.desc: Profiles of influential figures in the world of wood craftsmanship, focusing on famous furniture designer Thomas Chippendale. The film traces his journey from humble roots working as a joiner in Otley, West Yorkshire, to his success as a cabinet-maker in London

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: A hot summer swimming spot is the latest painting to take shape under the brush of American artist Bob Ross

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit archaeological digs across the country, beginning with a look at the work being carried out in the west of Britain. The presenters visit Salisbury Museum, finding out about developments at sites including the enormous lost Welsh city of Trellech and Kent's Cavern, a prehistoric cave where Britain's earliest human remains have been found

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross re-creates the enchanted timberlands of America just before nightfall

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: To celebrate Beethoven's 250th anniversary, Tom Service presents a concert in which conductor Nicholas Collon and the Aurora Orchestra unpick Beethoven's Seventh Symphony to explore the composer's creative genius, before performing the work from memory. Also on the bill at the Royal Albert Hall is a new work by British composer Richard Ayres, whose piece is inspired by Beethoven's struggle with his loss of hearing

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane explores the wealth of wildlife attracted to the Firth of Forth. Tessa Dunlop reveals how the Victorian zeal for cleanliness turned the Thames into a giant toilet bowl

programme.title: Goldstone (2016)
programme.desc: Detective Jay Swan's investigation of a young woman's disappearance causes friction in an Outback township under a mining company's sway. Local cop Josh Waters is growing used to looking the other way over everyday corruption, while Aboriginal elder Jimmy sees Jay has a deeper link to the territory. Thriller, starring Aaron Pedersen, Alex Russell, David Wenham and David Gulpill

programme.title: Opera Mums with Bryony Kimmings
programme.desc: Inspired by the lives of single mums and informed by opera experts at the English National Opera, performance artist Bryony Kimmings writes and directs her own operetta. The demands of the opera industry are turned on their head by Kimmings' insistence that real life is just as deserving of high art as the stories of the rich and powerful

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: An examination of how Fred Rose's 1877 Serio Comic Map of Europe at War allowed maps to take on a new direction and form, reflecting a changing world that meant they could be used as devices for humour, satire or story-telling. The graphic artist used a combination of creatures and human figures to represent each European nation, while in 1880 he was able to capture the mood of the British public with designs that commented on election issues featuring Gladstone and Disraeli

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross re-creates the enchanted timberlands of America just before nightfall

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary following workers at Rolls-Royce's Derby factory as they construct the company's Trent 1000 jet engine in preparation for its maiden flight attached to a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. The film explores the technology involved in its construction, and charts the ups and downs of working on the assembly line. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher

programme.title: Young, Gifted and Classical: The Making of a Maestro
programme.desc: Documentary about cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason, the first black performer to be named BBC Young Musician of the Year, and his six musically gifted siblings. The programme explores issues of diversity in the world of classical music and culminates in Sheku's biggest performance to date at London's Royal Festival Hall, appearing alongside Chineke!, an orchestra composed entirely of ethnic minority musicians

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Siblings Sheku and Isata Kanneh-Mason take to the stage for their first Prom together to present an evening of chamber music for cello and piano. Featuring works by Beethoven, Rachmaninov, American composer Samuel Barber, and English composer Frank Bridge, the brother and sister pairing bring their unique musical chemistry to the Royal Albert Hall. Presented by Tom Service, with guest Joanna MacGregor

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Memories of the late '60s and early '70s, a period when inequality, poverty and racism fanned the flames of radical black politics and resulted in a harder soul sound. Stax Records went from being a label built around an integrated house band to becoming a black-centric business spearheaded by Isaac Hayes' expansive, flamboyant soul symphonies. Meanwhile, the Temptations tackled socially aware subjects like the Detroit riot and absent fathers, while Marvin Gaye conceived the epic What's Going On. Narrated by Carleen Anderson, with contributions from Mavis Staples, David Porter, Al Bell, Otis Williams, Barrett Strong and Fred Wesley

programme.title: Motown at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of studio performances by some of Motown's greatest artists, originally broadcast to mark the record label's 50th anniversary. Featuring Diana Ross and the Supremes, the Temptations, Smokey Robinson and the Miracles, the Jackson Five, the Four Tops and Stevie Wonder

programme.title: BBC Proms 2017
programme.desc: At London's Royal Albert Hall, Jools Holland pays tribute to Stax Records, alongside original stars Eddie Floyd, Sam Moore, William Bell, Booker T Jones and Steve Cropper, plus UK artists including Tom Jones, James Morrison, Beverley Knight, Nadia Rose and Sweetie Irie, and Ruby Turner. Founded in 1957, the Memphis-based company was synonymous with Southern Soul, a distinctive blend of funk, gospel and R'n'B, producing soul artists such as Otis Redding, Carla Thomas, and Sam and Dave. Music includes Green Onions, Knock on Wood, Soul Man and Try a Little Tenderness

programme.title: Gregory Porter's Popular Voices
programme.desc: The soul and jazz performer explores the soft, intimate art of crooning. Born with the arrival of the microphone in the 1930s, crooning was initially about men seducing women and thrived through signature stars such as Bing Crosby, Nat King Cole and Frank Sinatra. However, far from disappearing with the advent of rock 'n' roll, the art of crooning gained a new existential edge and was transformed by the likes of Roy Orbison, David Bowie and even Lana Del Rey into a haunting and abiding strain of contemporary pop. Contributors include Iggy Pop, Joshua Homme, George Benson and Beck

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Memories of the late '60s and early '70s, a period when inequality, poverty and racism fanned the flames of radical black politics and resulted in a harder soul sound. Stax Records went from being a label built around an integrated house band to becoming a black-centric business spearheaded by Isaac Hayes' expansive, flamboyant soul symphonies. Meanwhile, the Temptations tackled socially aware subjects like the Detroit riot and absent fathers, while Marvin Gaye conceived the epic What's Going On. Narrated by Carleen Anderson, with contributions from Mavis Staples, David Porter, Al Bell, Otis Williams, Barrett Strong and Fred Wesley

programme.title: Young, Gifted and Classical: The Making of a Maestro
programme.desc: Documentary about cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason, the first black performer to be named BBC Young Musician of the Year, and his six musically gifted siblings. programme.desc: When Mr Crosswire gets a robot butler for Father's Day, Muffy fears Bailey may be replaced

programme.desc: The farmer prepares for a date by dusting off his prized hairpiece, but Shaun loses it while showing off to the flock and enlists Bitzer's aid to find it before the big night

programme.title: Nikki Lilly Bakes
programme.desc: Junior Bake Off winner Nikki Lilly creates a pizza muffin.

programme.title: Absolute Genius with Dick & Dom
programme.desc: Dick and Dom go head to head with the fiercest forces in nature as they enter the genius world of engineering against floods, wind and earthquakes. They head to the Netherlands to see how one of the world's great coastal defences keeps the sea at bay, find a suitably hair-raising method to put earthquake proofing to the test and tackle gale force winds in their bid to storm-proof their most precious possessions

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Historical characters and events are explored in sketches and quizzes based on the books by Terry Deary. Comedy talent including Sarah Hadland, Steve Punt and Meera Syal help bring the strange facts, gory battles and crazy inventions to life

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky begins to receive countless gifts from a mystery super-fan, and coincidentally, unusual things start to disappear from his flat

programme.desc: Shaun decides to try skateboarding and sets about building a skate park from scrap he finds in a dump

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson

Jermaine joins him for the final session before the new season starts, but will he be able to cope with the pressure and keep up with the high standards?

programme.title: Land Girls

Esther fears Nancy may be a Nazi collaborator and food rations officer Ernest Luckhurst arrives at Finch's farm intent on catching him out

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history of the Vegetarian Society and discover how the movement changed public attitudes and became part of the mainstream. They also consider how British gardening has influenced the nation's cuisine, and provide a few recipes along the way

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life in the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and a couple of gold plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Life at Colchester Zoo for curator Sarah Forsyth, who heads into the busiest summer of her life keeping her animals happy while four out of five of her staff are furloughed. The documentary follows Sarah for two weeks, entirely inside the walls of one of Britain's largest zoos, as she works tirelessly to deal with the summer baby boom, animal emergencies and unique situations created by the absence of paying public

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock. Silversmith Brenton West is on hand to fix a precious metal box that is tarnished, tattered and coming apart at the seams. Made of a delicate metal called antimony, it poses a real challenge for Brenton, who isn't helped when Steve warns him of his own youthful experience trying to repair antimony

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Just six weeks after his wedding, a 28-year-old man named Darren disappears, leaving Inspector Simon Orton and the team at Durham Police baffled. As the detectives dig deeper into the events leading up to his disappearance, they uncover a series of secrets and lies, and begin to realise all is not what it seems. Clare McElvaney and her partner from South Durham CID have to work out which information they have been given is relevant to their case, and which is not

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels off-base for the high society wedding of Abigail's cousin. Raelle throws herself into her relationship with Scylla, who, unbeknownst to Raelle, faces an impossible choice. Abigail becomes disillusioned with her privilege while amongst the notable Bellweather line. Tally gets hit with two life-altering revelations that change the very fabric of the unit

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail has to come to terms with her vulnerability in the aftermath of a horrific event. Tally struggles with being supportive, while also keeping a secret from her unit. Raelle takes extreme measures to connect with Scylla

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Shropshire, meeting officers who reveal how they are taking knives off the streets. Rav Wilding finds out what counter-terrorism experts advise in a crisis

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts looks round a one-bedroom flat in Tunbridge Wells and Martel Maxwell examines the state of a former supermarket building in Derbyshire and a mid-terrace in Liverpool

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Following a modern slavery team as they check for signs of workers being exploited. Plus, how an American Football team in the UK received a boost from proceeds of crime funding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright visits a rented terraced house that is overcrowded and riddled with cockroaches. He then visits North Ayrshire, where the council is cracking down on empty homes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham to take to auction in Sheffield. Anita also brings along a mystery object that has people scratching their heads

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Finch hires families displaced by the bombing as cheap labour, but when a break-in at the Manor House leads Lady Hoxley to order them out, the situation escalates into a riot. Bea does not have time to answer Billy's proposal as she goes into labour, and Joyce reveals the truth about the affair when the Hoxleys host a party

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the food provided by British waterways as they fly-fish for trout, prepare a meal of stuffed wild duck and discover how the Gloucestershire tradition of eating baby eels has virtually disappeared

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a married couple with a £500,000 budget find a romantic retirement home in the Dorset countryside. Nicki also meets members of the public committed to refurbishing the region's distinctive 'finger posts'

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson try to bring an Alpine-cum-Oktoberfest element to a couple's garden in Chesham, Buckinghamshire, to give Munich-born wife Lisa a taste of home

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture experts, horologists, metal workers, ceramicists and upholsterers restore more family heirlooms - featuring an art deco clock, a teddy bear and a 3D photograph viewer

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace consider some of the very best MasterChef highlights, from the most amazing dishes to outstanding challenges to some of the most impressive individual success stories. John and Gregg also reflect on what it takes to be 'the best', and how finalists and winners start to shine during the culinary rounds - in particular when in 2012 the semi-finalists took over the brasserie on a cross-channel ferry. Then the judges revisit one of the final challenges from 2008, when three celebrities faced cooking with Marcus Wareing - which resulted in one of the most memorable moments in Celebrity MasterChef history

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The second of a two-part story picks up where things left off last week, with Humphrey, Florence and Dwayne having swapped the sun and sand of the Caribbean for the cold and noise of London. Their latest investigation has taken them back to the detective inspector's home town - but they've hit a brick wall with the discovery of another dead body. And now they have two murders to solve. Meanwhile, Dwayne has an emotional encounter and Humphrey receives advice from an unexpected source that helps him reach a big decision. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall, Danny John-Jules and Josephine Jobert, with guest appearances from Ardal O'Hanlon and Sally Bretton

programme.title: I Am Not a Rapist
programme.desc: Of the 60,000 rape cases reported to police last year, it is estimated that up to eight per cent were false allegations. This documentary hears the stories of three young men who were falsely accused of rape, examining the consequences of the allegations on their lives and the lives of their loved ones

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Wales to take a behind-the-scenes look at the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, revealing how its crew members keep people safe on the seas. Plus, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a fairly modern two-bedroom flat in Manchester, there is a revisit to a property in Bexleyheath, Kent, that was first seen in 2012, and Dion Dublin is in Wolverhampton examining a semi-detached house

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Cameras follow drug raids across Lancashire and beyond. Plus, an impressive racing car is put up for sale at the proceeds-of-crime auction and the tale of how a woman helped police get the better of a romance fraudster in Durham is recounted. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Nancy is shocked when Ellen reveals Lawrence's dark secret. Annie is forced to choose between her duty to Harry and her love for Adam, Bea discovers Finch has doubts about her motives for marrying Billy, and Tucker's murderous revenge has devastating consequences for all four of the women. Drama, starring Summer Strallen and Nathaniel Parker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explain how necessity often forced cooks to make the most of limited ingredients to create tasty dishes. They reveal that many popular meals were invented during hard times, from mince and dumplings to Scotch broths

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Cornwall on a property search with a couple who have £750,000 to realise their dream of starting their married life together near the Cornish coast. While in the area, Nicki visits Looe Harbour to learn about the importance of sustainable fishing methods, which is championed by the local fishmongers she meets

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builder's rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention, spurring restorer Will Kirk into action. Also being given a much-needed makeover are an air-powered toy car and a RNLI donation box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer discovers the forgotten story of how Wales once led the world in copper production. He begins his quest in Anglesey on Parys Mountain, once the world's biggest copper mine, heading beneath its Mars-like landscape into the miles of tunnels that were first constructed in the 18th century. Then in south Wales, Will unearths the remains of Swansea's once huge copper industry, tracking down the final relics of the lost industrial world of Copperopolis. Later, Will cave-dives through a 19th-century flooded mine to discover its important role in Wales' industrial revolution

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian tries his hand at being a wedding planner. His task is to organise the Hollywood-themed big day of a young couple from the Welsh Valleys - but the bride is not fond of surprises, so what will she make of his chaotic contributions to cake design, music selection, ice sculpture and celebrity recruitment?

programme.title: Zodiac (2007)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime thriller about the tortuous hunt for a mysterious serial killer who terrorised San Francisco in the late 1960s and early 1970s, and taunted the police and media with cryptic messages. Three men became obsessed with identifying the murderer after his reign of terror inexplicably stopped, but their fixation with the case took a heavy toll on their lives. Starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Robert Downey Jr, Mark Ruffalo and Brian Cox

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock. Silversmith Brenton West is on hand to fix a precious metal box that is tarnished, tattered and coming apart at the seams. Made of a delicate metal called antimony, it poses a real challenge for Brenton, who isn't helped when Steve warns him of his own youthful experience trying to repair antimony

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Just six weeks after his wedding, a 28-year-old man named Darren disappears, leaving Inspector Simon Orton and the team at Durham Police baffled. As the detectives dig deeper into the events leading up to his disappearance, they uncover a series of secrets and lies, and begin to realise all is not what it seems. Clare McElvaney and her partner from South Durham CID have to work out which information they have been given is relevant to their case, and which is not

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels off-base for the high society wedding of Abigail's cousin. Raelle throws herself into her relationship with Scylla, who, unbeknownst to Raelle, faces an impossible choice. Abigail becomes disillusioned with her privilege while amongst the notable Bellweather line. Tally gets hit with two life-altering revelations that change the very fabric of the unit

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail has to come to terms with her vulnerability in the aftermath of a horrific event. Tally struggles with being supportive, while also keeping a secret from her unit. Raelle takes extreme measures to connect with Scylla

programme.title: Scottish Questions
programme.desc: Ministers are put on the spot by fellow MPs in their monthly question-and-answer session at Westminster

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.desc: Matt Allwright visits a rented terraced house that is overcrowded and riddled with cockroaches. He then visits North Ayrshire, where the council is cracking down on empty homes

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Rosa Bevan has come up with a way of using weeds in cooking and here she teaches viewers how to be wily with them

programme.title: First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Finch hires families displaced by the bombing as cheap labour, but when a break-in at the Manor House leads Lady Hoxley to order them out, the situation escalates into a riot. Bea does not have time to answer Billy's proposal as she goes into labour, and Joyce reveals the truth about the affair when the Hoxleys host a party

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the food provided by British waterways as they fly-fish for trout, prepare a meal of stuffed wild duck and discover how the Gloucestershire tradition of eating baby eels has virtually disappeared

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a married couple with a £500,000 budget find a romantic retirement home in the Dorset countryside. Nicki also meets members of the public committed to refurbishing the region's distinctive 'finger posts'

programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson try to bring an Alpine-cum-Oktoberfest element to a couple's garden in Chesham, Buckinghamshire, to give Munich-born wife Lisa a taste of home

programme.desc: Furniture experts, horologists, metal workers, ceramicists and upholsterers restore more family heirlooms - featuring an art deco clock, a teddy bear and a 3D photograph viewer
