programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace consider some of the very best MasterChef highlights, from the most amazing dishes to outstanding challenges to some of the most impressive individual success stories. John and Gregg also reflect on what it takes to be 'the best', and how finalists and winners start to shine during the culinary rounds - in particular when in 2012 the semi-finalists took over the brasserie on a cross-channel ferry. Then the judges revisit one of the final challenges from 2008, when three celebrities faced cooking with Marcus Wareing - which resulted in one of the most memorable moments in Celebrity MasterChef history

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The second of a two-part story picks up where things left off last week, with Humphrey, Florence and Dwayne having swapped the sun and sand of the Caribbean for the cold and noise of London. Their latest investigation has taken them back to the detective inspector's home town - but they've hit a brick wall with the discovery of another dead body. And now they have two murders to solve. Meanwhile, Dwayne has an emotional encounter and Humphrey receives advice from an unexpected source that helps him reach a big decision. Crime drama, starring Kris Marshall, Danny John-Jules and Josephine Jobert, with guest appearances from Ardal O'Hanlon and Sally Bretton

programme.title: I Am Not a Rapist
programme.desc: Of the 60,000 rape cases reported to police last year, it is estimated that up to eight per cent were false allegations. This documentary hears the stories of three young men who were falsely accused of rape, examining the consequences of the allegations on their lives and the lives of their loved ones

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Wales to take a behind-the-scenes look at the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, revealing how its crew members keep people safe on the seas. Plus, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a fairly modern two-bedroom flat in Manchester, there is a revisit to a property in Bexleyheath, Kent, that was first seen in 2012, and Dion Dublin is in Wolverhampton examining a semi-detached house

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Cameras follow drug raids across Lancashire and beyond. Plus, an impressive racing car is put up for sale at the proceeds-of-crime auction and the tale of how a woman helped police get the better of a romance fraudster in Durham is recounted. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the scandal of a north London bedsit that's simply not fit for purpose. He also joins the Birmingham empty homes officer who's fighting back against the city's 10,000 empty properties

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Nancy is shocked when Ellen reveals Lawrence's dark secret. Annie is forced to choose between her duty to Harry and her love for Adam, Bea discovers Finch has doubts about her motives for marrying Billy, and Tucker's murderous revenge has devastating consequences for all four of the women. Drama, starring Summer Strallen and Nathaniel Parker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explain how necessity often forced cooks to make the most of limited ingredients to create tasty dishes. They reveal that many popular meals were invented during hard times, from mince and dumplings to Scotch broths

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Cornwall on a property search with a couple who have £750,000 to realise their dream of starting their married life together near the Cornish coast. While in the area, Nicki visits Looe Harbour to learn about the importance of sustainable fishing methods, which is championed by the local fishmongers she meets

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builder's rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention, spurring restorer Will Kirk into action. Also being given a much-needed makeover are an air-powered toy car and a RNLI donation box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Laura Goodwin

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Rich brothers Harry and David head to Corby, Northamptonshire to meet Keri and Mark. The pair don't get to share too much time together, so though they love the pub, instead of going out they want to create a pub garden at home. Charlie and the siblings assess the pair's boring and featureless plot and come up with ideas as to how it can be turned into a relaxing and tranquil social space for Keri, Mark and their friends. With a whole garden to make over and a proper bar to incorporate, the challenge promises to be a tough task to pull off

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The Browns await the fruits of their fraud after faking Grandad's death to get a payout, but the insurance company starts to grow suspicious. Agnes struggles to keep him out of sight, as she has promised the money to Dermot and Maria, who are desperate to get a place of their own

programme.title: Zodiac (2007)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime thriller about the tortuous hunt for a mysterious serial killer who terrorised San Francisco in the late 1960s and early 1970s, and taunted the police and media with cryptic messages. Three men became obsessed with identifying the murderer after his reign of terror inexplicably stopped, but their fixation with the case took a heavy toll on their lives. Starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Robert Downey Jr, Mark Ruffalo and Brian Cox

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A Merseyside drug dealer has his BMW seized and sold off at auction. Meanwhile, police in Derby are after a man they suspect is part of a 'county lines' drug operation

programme.desc: Liverpool City Council's housing team investigate a rental property that is so dangerous for its residents it has to be immediately shut down

programme.desc: After three months, Annie is still struggling to come to terms with Harry's death, Nancy and Lawrence embark on a full-blown affair and Joyce faces a dilemma about her husband. programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history of the Vegetarian Society and discover how the movement changed public attitudes and became part of the mainstream. They also consider how British gardening has influenced the nation's cuisine, and provide a few recipes along the way

programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life in the Welsh borders. programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Life at Colchester Zoo for curator Sarah Forsyth, who heads into the busiest summer of her life keeping her animals happy while four out of five of her staff are furloughed. programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: The ex-footballer reflects on his upbringing in Belfast's Shankill district and his career at the top of his sport during the 1980s. Raised in Belfast's Shankill district, Norman went on to become the youngest player to score for Manchester United's first team, the youngest FA Cup scorer in history and the youngest ever footballer to play in World Cup finals. However, within a decade, his career was cut cruelly short. Speaking to Stephen Watson, Norman reveals the true story behind his sporting life - what it was like to have and then lose it all - and how he managed to rebuild his life. Featuring unseen archive material

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Deforestation has skyrocketed in Colombia since the peace deal of 2016. Our World joins a team of scientists from the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew as they go on a mission into virgin tropical rainforest. They hope to discover and save rare plant species before they are destroyed and vanish forever.

programme.title: Air Tòir Manachainn Dhèir (The Lost Monastery of Deer)
programme.desc: One of the most important texts in the Scottish Gaelic language, the Book of Deer was written in the ancient monastery of Deer that disappeared over a thousand years ago. This is the story of a team of archaeologists and volunteers who spent nine days hunting for the lost monastery of Deer, where the book was written. In the event, those involved found more than they imagined possible

programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Oli, Jan, Peter and Stella pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn admonishes Will, reminding him what happened last time he brought a gun into the house, while Cain notices the church door has been left open and finds Harriet inside sobbing. When Amy arrives at the Woolpack for her pizza tasting night she's hurt to discover Marlon has also invited others, and things get worse when Tracy arrives as there's an obvious frostiness between the two girls

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally and Faye try to download the deleted camera app and CCTV footage, while the police tell Geoff that new evidence has come to light and they need to ask him some more questions. David confides in Sarah that he worries Shona will never return to normal, and Nina calls to see Asha and gently suggests that she can do much better than Corey

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Staff at the airport deal with one of the UK's worst winter storms, with winds raging out on the airfield. Meanwhile, rookie passenger experience manager Lee finds himself dealing with his first emergency fire

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons
programme.desc: The best moments from Beverley Callard's career on Coronation Street, charting the key storylines and dramas that have made Liz McDonald such an iconic character. The programme also features archive footage from Beverley's big break on television in 1983, when she appeared on Emmerdale Farm, and the following year she appeared on Coronation Street alongside Gail Tilsley as a character called June Dewhurst. It would be another five years before she made her first appearance as army wife Liz, and Beverley reveals all about her early days on the soap and how she, and on-screen husband Jim were involved in the casting of their twins Steve and Andy

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer the questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from all three races in round four at Knockhill. With commentary from David Addison and Tim Harvey, and reports by Louise Goodman

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 11 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 167.5km route from Chatelaillon-Plage to Poitiers. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in Austin as he continues his culinary journey through Texas, where he samples vegan barbecue food from one of Austin's most famous food trucks, and cooks recipes using locally sourced ingredients. James also meets Austin native and musician Jackie Venson, to find out why the city is known as America's live music capital

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn admonishes Will, reminding him what happened last time he brought a gun into the house, while Cain notices the church door has been left open and finds Harriet inside sobbing. When Amy arrives at the Woolpack for her pizza tasting night she's hurt to discover Marlon has also invited others, and things get worse when Tracy arrives as there's an obvious frostiness between the two girls

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally and Faye try to download the deleted camera app and CCTV footage, while the police tell Geoff that new evidence has come to light and they need to ask him some more questions. David confides in Sarah that he worries Shona will never return to normal, and Nina calls to see Asha and gently suggests that she can do much better than Corey

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Staff at the airport deal with one of the UK's worst winter storms, with winds raging out on the airfield. Meanwhile, rookie passenger experience manager Lee finds himself dealing with his first emergency fire

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons
programme.desc: The best moments from Beverley Callard's career on Coronation Street, charting the key storylines and dramas that have made Liz McDonald such an iconic character. The programme also features archive footage from Beverley's big break on television in 1983, when she appeared on Emmerdale Farm, and the following year she appeared on Coronation Street alongside Gail Tilsley as a character called June Dewhurst. It would be another five years before she made her first appearance as army wife Liz, and Beverley reveals all about her early days on the soap and how she, and on-screen husband Jim were involved in the casting of their twins Steve and Andy

programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from all three races in round four at Knockhill. With commentary from David Addison and Tim Harvey, and reports by Louise Goodman

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill presents another edition of his popular programme, showcasing embarrassing home-videos and malfunctioning moments, filmed and submitted by loyal viewers. Clips feature flatulent selfies, misused contraceptives and a rather distracting wedding guest

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates another selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile phone footage. Featuring giddy grandmas, wedding nightmares and more below-the-belt mishaps

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy tries to make Mateo her new assistant, leading to accusations of favouritism and a group competition. Plus, Jonah tries to show Garrett he's one of the guys

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Sandra and Jerry are getting wed, but Jonah's attempt to help the caterers and Dina's obsession with security threaten to ruin things. Garrett struggles with his best man speech

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: When Liz spends the day with Mimi, Maria and Alex, she discovers a new revelation about Rosa. Meanwhile, Max takes aim at Wyatt

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Culinary-themed dating series. This time, Kirby from London chooses three men to date based on the menus they have put together. After the dates are done and dusted, she can only choose to see one of them again, but who will it be?

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including a quad bike that ruins a wedding, an agoraphobic dog and a sensitive cat that hates being called fat

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a star-studded version of the game show, asking Jennie McAlpine, Jason Watkins and Ore Oduba to 'say what they see' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The host chats to rock star Chris Martin, who then joins his Coldplay colleagues to perform Cry, Cry, Cry from their album Everyday Life. Plus, Zach Woods discusses Avenue 5

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Volunteers from Ellen Degeneres' studio audience play her outrageously fun games, including Son of a One-Eyed Monster, Taste Buds, Blindfolded Musical Chairs and Scary Go Round

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Group C of the celebrities hit the stage as Night Angel, Bear, Astronaut, T-Rex, Rhino and Swan try to impress Jenny McCarthy, Robin Thicke, Nicole Scherzinger and Ken Jeong

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, three more singletons hope to win a date with their style savviness. Hot tub salesman Jack has no information to go on but the outfits that have been bought

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Scott, a mannequin sculptor from London, Leeds-based dentist Keshav, Newcastle electrician Cory and Adam, a labourer from Bedfordshire enter the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing Paddy McGuinness's 30 single women. However, to win a date they must first convince the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Plus, Paddy provides an update on last week's couples from the Isle of Fernandos

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan moves out after he and Walden get into a fight over a broken toaster, only for Walden to feel bad and try to apologise when Alan moves in with Herb. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Ryan Stiles

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Herb goes on a wild party binge at the beach house, prompting Walden and Alan to feel the need to keep up with him. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher and Jon Cryer

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: US version of the hit reality show featuring a gang of gorgeous young singles gathered in one villa for the summer of their lives

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris is named as homecoming king, but Meg learns that the cool kids plan to prank him during the ceremony, while Peter gets jealous when Cleveland starts hanging out with Jerome

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins struggle financially, but then Stewie suddenly becomes a star after appearing in an advertisement for peanut butter

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Carter go to Africa to get their money back after they are swindled in an e-mail scam, while Brian pursues Meg's friend Patty

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan is rude to an old man in a wheelchair, who retaliates by casting a spell that makes him age rapidly. Vowing to redeem himself, the CIA agent determines to climb Kilimanjaro to reverse the curse put on him. With the voice of Patrick Stewart

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan persuades his last single friend to get married so he can realise his dream of becoming a best man, but when he invites an ex-convict to the wedding, she jeopardises the event. Elsewhere, Ricky Martin causes Roger to doubt himself

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland takes some troubled youngsters under his wing in an effort to help them get back on the right track. With Cleveland Jr's help, he creates a club for the group called the Crazy Eights - but things do not turn out as planned

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The host is joined iby actress Aubrey Plaza, who talks about the Film Independent Spirit Awards, and Jamie Foxx takes on Ellen's Burning Questions. Plus, Coldplay perform Orphans from the album Everyday Life

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile phone footage

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a cat that uses a fork to eat, a robotic babysitter and mad parking with a monster truck

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy organises a community service event, but it's jeopardized by Jeff's return and Glenn's desire to help his co-workers. Sandra confronts Jonah about his strategy for soliciting donations, whileGarrett tries to prove his intelligence to Dina

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: When Amy has to bring her baby to the store, she grows frustrated with people's criticisms of her parenting and Glenn enlists Jerusha to help. Garrett helps Dina plan her first date with her boyfriend, while Sandra and Cheyenne hatch a conspiracy theory

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: Alex and Kyle investigate what Sgt Manes is hiding. Max, Michael, Liz and Maria visit a faith healer in Texas. Sci-fi drama, starring Jeanine Mason

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Sasha from London is asked to choose which three blind dates from a selection of five potential suitors she would like to get to know better, based entirely on dinner menus they have created

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates a selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile-phone footage, featuring dogs that can drive tractors and administer CPR, as well as a thieving blackbird, snowfalls, ball games and playground problems

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking Rob Beckett, Ruth Jones and Jordan Banjo to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Grace and Frankie stars Jane Fonda and Lily Tomlin are the guest hosts, joined by their co-stars Sam Waterson and Michael Sheen, and Milo Ventimilgia, who discusses This Is Us

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Volunteers from Ellen's studio audience play her outrageously fun games including Son of a One-Eyed Monster, You Bet Your Wife, Say Whaaat?, and Mt Saint Ellen

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Night Angel, Astronaut, T-Rex and Rhino perform for guest judge Will Arnett and the rest of the panel as they are whittled down to three. Nick Cannon presents as one of the singers is unmasked and sent home

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in Manchester, where they try to win the affections of fashion stylist Courtney by each buying him an outfit

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness invites more would-be Romeos to enters the 'love lift' and claim the hearts of his 30 single women. This time, the men seeking to win a date are Louis, a debt collector from Great Yarmouth, Tenby-based tugboat skipper Patrick, Thomas, a chorister from Arundel, and Oxford stunt-man Jon. Each will be seeking a companion to whisk off to the Isle of Fernandos, but first they must get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates another selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile phone footage, featuring a ship's launch going spectacularly wrong, a toddler answering back to the taxman, and the ultimate drooling dog

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan is still down in the dumps after his split from Lyndsey, so Walden decides to give him a makeover. He then faces a moral dilemma when he attracts the attention of a married woman

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden has a dilemma when he finds he is more attracted to the grandmother of a beautiful-but-ditzy young woman than the woman herself. Meanwhile, Jake reveals that he is being transferred to Japan, so Alan decides that a road trip is in order

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: Host Arielle Vandenberg and narrator Matthew Hoffman preside over the antics as a bunch of gorgeous young singles embark on a summer of potential romance in Las Vegas - sequestered in a 'bubble' at a stunning villa on top of Caesars Entertainment's boutique hotel, The Cromwell. The candidates able to stand the test of time and make it all the way to the final will eventually have the chance of bagging a $50,000 cash prize

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian form a children's band that quickly rises to fame until they let a girl get between them and drive the band apart. Meanwhile, Peter and Lois insist that Chris gets a job, so he takes a job managing Quagmire's sex life as his personal assistant

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter infuriates Lois by turning the attic into a man cave, while Brian builds a device to help Stewie conquer his terrifying nightmares

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger acts as the Smith family therapist under the guise of his alter ego Dr Penguin, but his services are cut short when he is sent to fight in the Iraq War - leaving Klaus with the opportunity to take over his counselling duties

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A jealous Francine tries to break up Stan's renewed relationship with a former girlfriend, only to unwittingly leave Roger at the mercy of an alien-hunter. Elsewhere, Steve accidentally wears Hayley's panties to school, and finds they bring him luck

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland is shocked to discover that the object of his father's affections - Donna's Auntie Momma - is really a man, as he and his new family celebrate their first Thanksgiving together. Meanwhile, Roberta plans to spend the day with Federline

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland Jr and Roberta go on a school trip, leaving Cleveland and Donna excited by the prospect of having the house to themselves. However, Rallo does his best to keep the couple apart

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan is still down in the dumps after his split from Lyndsey, so Walden decides to give him a makeover. He then faces a moral dilemma when he attracts the attention of a married woman

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: Frustrated by her inability to remember what happened the night Rosa died, Isobel turns to Liz for help. Max and Cameron investigate a series of strange deaths in the town, while Sheriff Valenti reveals information to Kyle about his father. Drama, starring Jeanine Mason and Nathan Parsons

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson guides viewers through some more weird and wonderful Guinness World Records, including the fastest time to pull an aircraft, the most watermelons punched in one minute and the longest time to sing suspended upside down

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is worried when Nick won't talk about his life in London. Claire tells Des he'll have to give away some things from his single life

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin suggests to Gail that they think again about Canada. Tricia is furious when Sally won't let her do overtime. Carol brings Becky a framed photo of Jeff

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is worried when Biff is missing all night. Frank is shocked when he receives a phone call - and it's Kim on the other end. Mandy and Paddy share an intimate moment

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar finds himself in an awkward position when the police investigate a warehouse robbery. A lost property clear-out at the police station gives David a new hobby, but he soon manages to cause trouble. Starring Derek Fowlds and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike and Sally plan a holiday, but things run far from smoothly when Sid gets involved with the financial planning. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper discovers a cheap holiday scheme that requires the couple to join the Conservative Party - but George is a staunch Labour supporter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates a suspicious death with the help of CID and Walker begins to suspect a poacher may be involved. Vernon tempts fate by employing a new mechanic for the garage without asking Bernie and attempts to recreate a traditional dance at the town fete. Guest starring Hannah Gordon

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day one of the St Leger Meeting from Doncaster, featuring five races including the 2.10 Scarborough Stakes and 3.15 Sceptre Stakes. Plus, two races from Kempton Park. With analysis from Kevin Blake and Jason Weaver, reports by Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is concerned when Frank doesn't pick her up from the airport. Kathy goes to see Nick in prison. Mandy is hurt when Paddy makes jokes about her family

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe feels guilty for not being there when her father needed her. Mandy is accused of shoplifting. Biff wonders what will happen to Frank's estate following his death

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is furious to receive a demand from Liz's solicitor. Deirdre is thrilled when Stephen asks her out for dinner. Stephen sees the Platts off as they fly to Canada

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally finds it hard adjusting to being a boss. Deirdre worries that Stephen only took her out out of pity. Kevin tells Tricia that Sally stood up for and stopped Mike sacking her

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates a suspicious death with the help of CID and Walker begins to suspect a poacher may be involved. Vernon tempts fate by employing a new mechanic for the garage without asking Bernie and attempts to recreate a traditional dance at the town fete. Guest starring Hannah Gordon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: When potential witness Claire Olssen is intimidated into changing her statement, the investigation team look for alternative ways to reinforce their case. As Steve returns to work, Kelly is offered a deal that tests his resolve

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean informs Sid she wants a lover to bring a bit of spice back into her life. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland and Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers suffer a domestic crisis when Mildred discovers George has been writing to another woman. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy is forced to confront painful memories when he is subjected to a series of attacks and Walker begins to suspect a recently released prisoner may be involved. Lord Ashfordly's visiting relatives receive a cool reception and Bernie is shocked to find Rosie working at the garage, but impressed once he discovers her lucrative new venture. Starring Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Vanessa Hehir

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day two of the St Leger Meeting from Doncaster, including the 1.40 Nursery Handicap, 2.10 May Hill Stakes and 3.15 Park Hill Stakes. With analysis from Kevin Blake and Jason Weaver, reports by Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie tries to get close to Zoe. Zak writes a tribute song to Frank Tate. Chris is in a bad way following Frank's death, but he's speechless when Kim turns up at his house

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe turns on Kim and demands to know whose body was found in the quarry. Kim is surprised to learn her murder inquiry is still open. Jack avoids facing Andy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia apologises to Sally. Maureen goes on a blind date. Stephen is embarrassed when Alma questions him over his relationship with Deirdre

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera lends Tricia her support when she goes for a scan at the hospital. Maxine dreams of hitting the big time. Maud gets Bill to take her and Maureen to the pictures

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy is forced to confront painful memories when he is subjected to a series of attacks and Walker begins to suspect a recently released prisoner may be involved. Lord Ashfordly's visiting relatives receive a cool reception and Bernie is shocked to find Rosie working at the garage, but impressed once he discovers her lucrative new venture. Starring Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Vanessa Hehir

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Mercer and his associates are tried for their roles in the killing of 11-year-old Rhys Jones, but the verdict fails to bring closure for the boy's parents Melanie and Steve as the fractures in their relationship come to a head. Meanwhile an unexpected setback leaves Det Supt Dave Kelly with a tough decision to make. The concluding part of the fact-based drama centred on the devastating impact of a child's murder amid a wave of gang violence in Liverpool, starring Stephen Graham, Sinead Keenan and Brían F O'Byrne

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Another chance to see the feature-length first episode of the popular detective drama. Inspector Jack Frost's investigation into the case of a missing child uncovers a skeleton chained to a locked strongbox that tantalisingly turns out to be empty, but provides evidence of a crime committed 30 years previously. David Jason stars as the irascible detective, with Bruce Alexander, Matt Bardock and Claire Hackett

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Cash-strapped George knows exactly what he can sell to raise money - but decides not to tell Mildred. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton and Walker investigate a security van raid in Ashfordly and are convinced there is a link to a recent robbery at a local airfield, but a senior officer visiting from Middlesbrough dismisses their claims. Vernon takes a sudden interest in the flower show when he discovers David might be the owner of a rare, potentially prize-winning fuchsia and turns to Blaketon and Ventress for help when the plant suddenly disappears

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day three of the St Leger Meeting from Doncaster, featuring five races including the 1.40 Mallard Handicap, 2.10 Flying Childers Stakes and 3.15 Doncaster Cup. With analysis from Kevin Blake and Jason Weaver, reports by Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is furious that the police let Kim go without charging her. Andy and the Sugdens have a meeting with the headmistress. Kim makes Sophie doubt Frank's innocence

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The coffin containing the mystery body is exhumed. Sophie tells Kim that she needs to get to know James again. DI Cooke arrives just as Chris threatens Kim

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken is asked to direct the school play after the drama teacher falls ill. Jim heckles Liz in the street over money. Tricia assures the Duckworths she's finished with Carl

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fred offers Alec the chance to join Square Dealers. Ken struggles directing A Midsummer Night's Dream. Maud is stunned when Maureen tells her about her blind date

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton and Walker investigate a security van raid in Ashfordly and are convinced there is a link to a recent robbery at a local airfield, but a senior officer visiting from Middlesbrough dismisses their claims. Vernon takes a sudden interest in the flower show when he discovers David might be the owner of a rare, potentially prize-winning fuchsia and turns to Blaketon and Ventress for help when the plant suddenly disappears

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa decides to leave for Spain with baby James and says an awkward goodbye to Martin, but when he finds the scan of her brain, he spots a malformation and realises she could suffer a haemorrhage if she gets on the plane. Will the doctor make it to the airport in time? Meanwhile, Margaret reveals the real reason she has come to Portwenn, and disaster strikes at Bert and Jennifer's engagement party when Caroline receives an electric shock

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: With Louisa visiting her mother in Spain to get some perspective on their marriage, Martin knows he needs to try to change for the sake of their relationship, so he endeavours to keep his promise to her to see a therapist. Aunt Ruth arranges an appointment for him with Dr Rachel Timoney, but a lifeboat training exercise causes problems for the doctor. Will he get back in time for his first session? Martin Clunes stars

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Cabbot investigates when a woman is found murdered on the moors. As the identity of the victim is established, it becomes apparent any number of suspects may have had a reason to want her dead. Meanwhile, Banks is called in when the body of a teenage girl is discovered in a storage room down an alleyway. Stephen Tompkinson and Andrea Lowe star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth

programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: When potential witness Claire Olssen is intimidated into changing her statement, the investigation team look for alternative ways to reinforce their case. As Steve returns to work, Kelly is offered a deal that tests his resolve

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally finds it hard adjusting to being a boss. Deirdre worries that Stephen only took her out out of pity. Kevin tells Tricia that Sally stood up for and stopped Mike sacking her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is concerned when Frank doesn't pick her up from the airport. Kathy goes to see Nick in prison. Mandy is hurt when Paddy makes jokes about her family

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe feels guilty for not being there when her father needed her. Mandy is accused of shoplifting. Biff wonders what will happen to Frank's estate following his death

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates a suspicious death with the help of CID and Walker begins to suspect a poacher may be involved. Vernon tempts fate by employing a new mechanic for the garage without asking Bernie and attempts to recreate a traditional dance at the town fete. Guest starring Hannah Gordon

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean informs Sid she wants a lover to bring a bit of spice back into her life. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland and Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers suffer a domestic crisis when Mildred discovers George has been writing to another woman. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy is forced to confront painful memories when he is subjected to a series of attacks and Walker begins to suspect a recently released prisoner may be involved. Lord Ashfordly's visiting relatives receive a cool reception and Bernie is shocked to find Rosie working at the garage, but impressed once he discovers her lucrative new venture. Starring Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Vanessa Hehir

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day two of the St Leger Meeting from Doncaster, including the 1.40 Nursery Handicap, 2.10 May Hill Stakes and 3.15 Park Hill Stakes. With analysis from Kevin Blake and Jason Weaver, reports by Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie tries to get close to Zoe. Zak writes a tribute song to Frank Tate. Chris is in a bad way following Frank's death, but he's speechless when Kim turns up at his house

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe turns on Kim and demands to know whose body was found in the quarry. Kim is surprised to learn her murder inquiry is still open. Jack avoids facing Andy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia apologises to Sally. Maureen goes on a blind date. Stephen is embarrassed when Alma questions him over his relationship with Deirdre

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera lends Tricia her support when she goes for a scan at the hospital. Maxine dreams of hitting the big time. Maud gets Bill to take her and Maureen to the pictures

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy is forced to confront painful memories when he is subjected to a series of attacks and Walker begins to suspect a recently released prisoner may be involved. Lord Ashfordly's visiting relatives receive a cool reception and Bernie is shocked to find Rosie working at the garage, but impressed once he discovers her lucrative new venture. Starring Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Vanessa Hehir

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The headmaster of a local school is murdered, his body found pinned to the wall in the manner of his own butterfly collection. The dead man was a mysterious international club for geniuses, which are currently testing the school's pupils for possible new recruits. Barnaby and Winter investigate the secretive organisation, which has proven controversial in the local area, but also uncover the possibility of a connection between the killing and the impact of pesticides on a rare species of butterfly

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Mercer and his associates are tried for their roles in the killing of 11-year-old Rhys Jones, but the verdict fails to bring closure for the boy's parents Melanie and Steve as the fractures in their relationship come to a head. Meanwhile an unexpected setback leaves Det Supt Dave Kelly with a tough decision to make. The concluding part of the fact-based drama centred on the devastating impact of a child's murder amid a wave of gang violence in Liverpool, starring Stephen Graham, Sinead Keenan and Brían F O'Byrne

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Another chance to see the feature-length first episode of the popular detective drama. Inspector Jack Frost's investigation into the case of a missing child uncovers a skeleton chained to a locked strongbox that tantalisingly turns out to be empty, but provides evidence of a crime committed 30 years previously. David Jason stars as the irascible detective, with Bruce Alexander, Matt Bardock and Claire Hackett

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton and Walker investigate a security van raid in Ashfordly and are convinced there is a link to a recent robbery at a local airfield, but a senior officer visiting from Middlesbrough dismisses their claims. Vernon takes a sudden interest in the flower show when he discovers David might be the owner of a rare, potentially prize-winning fuchsia and turns to Blaketon and Ventress for help when the plant suddenly disappears

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day three of the St Leger Meeting from Doncaster, featuring five races including the 1.40 Mallard Handicap, 2.10 Flying Childers Stakes and 3.15 Doncaster Cup. With analysis from Kevin Blake and Jason Weaver, reports by Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is furious that the police let Kim go without charging her. Andy and the Sugdens have a meeting with the headmistress. Kim makes Sophie doubt Frank's innocence

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The coffin containing the mystery body is exhumed. Sophie tells Kim that she needs to get to know James again. DI Cooke arrives just as Chris threatens Kim

programme.desc: Ken is asked to direct the school play after the drama teacher falls ill. Jim heckles Liz in the street over money. Tricia assures the Duckworths she's finished with Carl

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa decides to leave for Spain with baby James and says an awkward goodbye to Martin, but when he finds the scan of her brain, he spots a malformation and realises she could suffer a haemorrhage if she gets on the plane. Will the doctor make it to the airport in time? Meanwhile, Margaret reveals the real reason she has come to Portwenn, and disaster strikes at Bert and Jennifer's engagement party when Caroline receives an electric shock

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: With Louisa visiting her mother in Spain to get some perspective on their marriage, Martin knows he needs to try to change for the sake of their relationship, so he endeavours to keep his promise to her to see a therapist. Aunt Ruth arranges an appointment for him with Dr Rachel Timoney, but a lifeboat training exercise causes problems for the doctor. Will he get back in time for his first session? Martin Clunes stars

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Cabbot investigates when a woman is found murdered on the moors. As the identity of the victim is established, it becomes apparent any number of suspects may have had a reason to want her dead. Meanwhile, Banks is called in when the body of a teenage girl is discovered in a storage room down an alleyway. Stephen Tompkinson and Andrea Lowe star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth

programme.desc: More of the most memorable moments from the past five years, looking back at some of Chester's more wily residents that have proven themselves to be expert escapologists. When the Sumatran orangutans are moved to a new home, keepers discover on CCTV that four of the troupe have gone missing. Meanwhile, three-month-old Andean bear cub Madidi escapes into the paddock next door

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Documentary following life inside an A&E department, focusing on patients and their families, as well as the work of the staff dealing with the emergencies. This episode follows the stories of three patients and how they cope when life takes an unexpected turn, including a patient who severed three of his fingers with an electric saw. Elsewhere, a man with motor neurone disease is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis, and a teenager struggles to come to terms with the impact of serious injuries he sustained in a car crash six weeks ago

programme.title: Can We Cure Kids' Cancer?
programme.desc: Following three children and their families as they undergo both cutting-edge and traditional treatments at the Royal Marsden in London. Cameras follow three-year-old Charlotte, who is one of the first children in the UK to take a pioneering new targeted drug that has saved her life, while toddler Artemis must undergo a stem cell transplant, first done over 50 years ago, as this is the best option available to her. Elsewhere Lily, who is 14, takes on a cocktail of chemotherapy and newer immunotherapy drugs

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: With passenger numbers sky rocketing, airports and planes are increasingly becoming pressurised environments. Footage captured by people who were there highlights some of the most dramatic, extraordinary and unusual moments that UK passengers have experienced. From terrible behaviour in the terminal and tantrums on the tarmac to full-scale brawls on board, the programme hears from the people who were involved. Expert analysis from industry insiders and psychologists explains why people behave how they do on flights from hell

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

Tennis fans John and Suzy bring the week to a close - can they serve up an ace menu and walk away with the prize?

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: With the majority of under-30s struggling to get on the property ladder and millions of over-60s sitting on empty rooms, young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together and seeing how they get along. In this edition, 21-year-old poet Marvell leaves his east London council house to lodge with retired couple Lynne and John in their converted chapel in the Somerset town of Glastonbury. Meanwhile, in Birmingham, 20-year-old Sophie co-habits with no-nonsense pensioner Eunice. How she will cope living under her landlord's strict house rules remains to be seen

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: The chances of a 25 to 35-year-old person owning a home have halved in the last 20 years, while millions of over-sixties are sitting on empty rooms, so here young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together. In this third instalment, dog groomer Liah-Rose moves home and business to Reading and pensioner landlords Carolynne and David's high-octane lifestyle. programme.title: Gogglebox

This latest run is scheduled to extend for the next 15 weeks

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Control Enthusiast
programme.desc: A stand-up performance by the comedian, including topics such as road rage, IBS, her favourite word and how she lets her husband know 'tonight's the night'. There are also stories about 'Rescue Men', passing wind in hospital pants, what can happen at a bra fitting and how she feels about her belly

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Recruitment consultant and boxing ring girl Chloe sits down with 'geezer' Anthony, who believes his cheeky wink can charm any woman. Actor Josh seeks a leading lady, but as a dwarf he has struggled to find someone who won't judge him on his height. His date is housing support officer Lori, who at four foot 11 has also found love a challenge. Also, dedicated father Rob is paired with former Butlin's Redcoat Donna. How will she react when he reveals he has 10 children?

programme.title: Table 19 (2017)
programme.desc: Dumped by the best man via text, a former maid of honour decides to pitch up at her friend's wedding anyway. She finds herself relegated to table 19, where her tablemates include a nanny, an openly warring married couple, a teenager seeking a date and a gangly Englishman who's clearly hiding a secret. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Craig Robinson, Lisa Kudrow and Stephen Merchant

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hair and beauty tutor April Richards hosts the first dinner party in Birmingham. She plans a starter of zebra meat, but the dish does not go down well with employment adviser Paz Thakrar, who is a strict vegetarian and thinks that even eating vegetables is cruel

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Scientist John Fennel hosts the second party in Birmingham, and decides to prepare an ambitious menu that makes use of his skills in the lab. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Close friends Janet Perrie and Fiona Hope moved to the South Ayrshire village of Dipple in search of an idyllic new life for themselves and their 22 dogs. Unfortunately for them, other residents were enraged by the noise, smell and mess and wasted no time in contacting the authorities. In a cul-de-sac in Leicestershire, the Pollard, Walne and Elliott families have been harassed by 68-year-old Leslie Collins for more than a decade after they complained about the noise from an illegal wood-waste business he was running from his home. He then began to target them with increasingly intimidating behaviour - including mooning, two-finger salutes, shouting abuse, cutting holes in hedges and spying on them with binoculars

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell tells the story behind the design and construction of Isambard Kingdom Brunel's famous bridge over the picturesque Avon gorge at Bristol, and discovers that some people question whether it was entirely the product of his own devising. The bridge remained unfinished in Brunel's lifetime, but when he died in 1859, his friends decided that the bridge should be completed as a memorial, using the chains from another of his suspension bridges that was being demolished

programme.title: Wild Britain
programme.desc: Revealing the hidden lives of the familiar and the more unusual animals in the United Kingdom. Cameras follow barn owls as they hunt for small mammals through the British countryside, and a heath potter wasp builds an intricate clay pot for her young, captured in its entirety for the very first time. Plus, the farmers doing what they can to help our countryside creatures

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: A wife accompanies her husband to the doctor explaining that he has had bad breath just recently - and when that's sorted, she takes the opportunity for the GP to also take a look at a massive bruise on her back. A patient with lung cancer arrives for a check-up with Dr Ashraf, nurse Emma Rogers meets Hazel, who wants a new contraceptive implant, and a man comes in concerned about his weight loss

programme.title: Elephant Hospital
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dr Paul O'Donoghue joins the expert vets in Thailand at the world's largest hospital devoted solely to treating elephants. A night-time emergency stretches the hospital as three sick female elephants from the same sanctuary are admitted with colic. One of the patients arrives with her four-month-old baby, who stays while her mother receives treatment, but it proves to be a massive task to nurse these big patients back to health

programme.title: Extreme Nightmare Tenants
programme.desc: The work of lawyer Chris Sharpe, who specialises in helping landlords who are plagued by troublesome tenants and trespassers. Chris tries to remove trespassers from a landowner's one-and-a-half-acre site, filling it with up to 20 caravans and a mountain of rubbish - only for things to take a menacing turn. Business partners in Bristol dream of turning a derelict swimming baths into flats, but their dreams turns sour when squatters move in, and Chris receives a shock when he tries to evict squatters from an abandoned print factory in Essex

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges

But in May 1970, four youths set light to the famous bridge, and Rob meets a fireman who fought the blaze that fateful night
