programme.desc: Rose Matafeo, Rachel Parris, Romesh Ranganathan and Jon Richardson are posed increasingly absurd hypothetical situations. Presented by Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Terms and Conditions Apply
programme.desc: Using his trademark, powerpoint-comedy and mischievous games, Dave invites David O'Doherty, Rose Matafeo and Ed Gamble to help him make sense of the world

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian and actor David Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical current affairs quiz from October 2016, with Labour MP Chris Bryant and New Zealand comedian Rose Matafeo joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's news

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: This time, Jon is joined by Victoria Coren-Mitchell and Rob Beckett as they attempt to analyse and assess the things that worry them the most about the food that we eat

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies invites all five contestants to have some global fun with a beach ball, while Lou Sanders has some wise words for the youth of today. Plus, Paul Sinha tries to invent a revolutionary new use for a basketball, and Sian Gibson attempts to rescue Little Alex Horne from the sharp beak of an aquatic bird

programme.title: Dara O Briain's Go 8 Bit
programme.desc: Team captains Steve McNeil and Sam Pamphilon are joined by celebrity guests Sara Cox and Daniel Sloss who lock horns on FIFA 17, Wipeout and Crossy Road

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc takes over the reins for the extended edition of the topical quiz, with comedian Hal Cruttenden and writer Sathnam Sanghera joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Unearthing WWII
programme.desc: David O'Keefe and Wayne Abbott visit Monte Cassino in Italy, searching for evidence of a bloody campaign near the Abbey and investigating the story behind two artefacts

programme.title: From World War to Cold War
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring the end of the Second World War, and how the plans for the future agreed by the Allied nations at the Yalta Conference in 1945 broke down

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: The unsolved murders committed by the Butcher of Kingsbury Run, a serial killer operating in Cleveland, Ohio, in the 1930s. The Butcher killed at least 12 men and women, dismembering their bodies and scattering them across the city

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs' classic documentary explores the Second World War's powerful legacy. For many people the conflict was the most significant experience of their lives, but it also left millions dead - ordinary civilians and victims of persecution, as well as military casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Tewkesbury Abbey, Gloucestershire. A visitor from Australia hopes a set of silver knives will be worth the cost of her plane ticket, while two sisters who split their great great grandmother's jewellery between them hope to find out who has the most valuable inheritance. Lennox Cato values a box that may have belonged to William Shakespeare's wife, and a collection of rare figures and dolls turns out to be a find of national importance

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A formerly luxurious and strategic mountain-top villa on the island of Rhodes in the Mediterranean that's been ravaged by acts of revenge by locals since 1955

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a new journey following his Bradshaw's handbook from south-west Wales to Cambridge, beginning in Pembroke Dock, where Queen Victoria's royal yachts were built. In the little market town of Naberth he investigates what caused rebels to dress up as women, later spending the night at an inn in Carmarthen where Horatio Nelson once met Emma Hamilton. Next day, the former politician pitches in with the volunteers who look after the Gwili Heritage line and ends his leg in Swansea, where he learns how to pose for a photograph in Victorian style

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins in the ruinous gardens at Aberglasney House near Llandeilo before riding shotgun in the driver's cab on one of the most scenic routes in Britain. Over the border in Leominster, Herefordshire, he steps out onto the dance floor at the Lion Hotel Ballroom, where a grand ball was held to celebrate the opening of the Ludlow to Hereford railway, and finishes this leg of his journey at a traditional cider house in Hereford

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Audley End, Essex, where experts value a sword that played an important role in the end of the American War of Independence, a collection of toilet chains and a silver container that once contained a goat's gallstone believed to have magical powers. The biggest shock of the day comes when Eric Knowles reveals the worth of a set of three vases decorated with fairies

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth sets out on its first transatlantic crossing, a four-month deployment on the high seas which has the primary purpose of putting the top secret F-35B Lightning Stealth Fighter to the test. Within hours of leaving Portsmouth, the vessel encounters a Russian warship and needs to take immediate evasive action, and the crew has to deal with floods and dangerous fuel leaks on their way to Florida

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: The crew visits Germany to collect a multi-million-pound maintenance machine so large it will need to be loaded onto two trucks. Eric and Chris oversee a diesel-engine exchange

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A strange-looking town with a supernatural atmosphere, a collection of imposing buildings in southern Australia, and the remains of curious-looking basins emerging from the waves

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan
programme.desc: A look at Hitler's plans to build ambitious new cities in the countries he conquered, as well as schemes to transform Berlin to serve as a global capital city

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The untold secret story of war production that shaped the Second World War, beginning with how the German aviation industry came to dominate the skies of Europe

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins underground at Big Pit coal mine in Blaenavon, where he discovers how Victorians toiled night and day to power the Industrial Revolution. On the River Usk, he learns about 19th-century developments in angling before heading to Ascott-under-Wychwood in Oxfordshire - once the scene of a farm labourers' dispute that ended in rioting. Michael concludes this leg of his journey at Blenheim Palace

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning in the heart of academia in Oxford, Michael Portillo visits the Bodleian library to see some Victorian treasures, including Mary Shelley's Frankenstein manuscript and a pocket-sized edition of Bradshaw's Companion. At Bicester, he investigates two new rail projects, finds out about Victorian philanthropy in Bedford and finishes in Luton, where he explores the dark arts of the hat-maker

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Lancaster factory, one of the biggest buildings in Europe at the time, helped to create a truly war-winning weapon

programme.title: Open All Hours

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: In 1879, widow Julia Martha Thomas was killed in her own home by her servant. The death of struck at the heart of Victorian society, at the sanctity of the home and the rigid divides between the classes, for Kate Webster did not only kill and dismember her employer - she stole her clothes, her property and her identity

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Audley End, Essex, where experts value a sword that played an important role in the end of the American War of Independence, a collection of toilet chains and a silver container that once contained a goat's gallstone believed to have magical powers. The biggest shock of the day comes when Eric Knowles reveals the worth of a set of three vases decorated with fairies

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A strange-looking town with a supernatural atmosphere, a collection of imposing buildings in southern Australia, and the remains of curious-looking basins emerging from the waves

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins underground at Big Pit coal mine in Blaenavon, where he discovers how Victorians toiled night and day to power the Industrial Revolution. On the River Usk, he learns about 19th-century developments in angling before heading to Ascott-under-Wychwood in Oxfordshire - once the scene of a farm labourers' dispute that ended in rioting. Michael concludes this leg of his journey at Blenheim Palace

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning in the heart of academia in Oxford, Michael Portillo visits the Bodleian library to see some Victorian treasures, including Mary Shelley's Frankenstein manuscript and a pocket-sized edition of Bradshaw's Companion. At Bicester, he investigates two new rail projects, finds out about Victorian philanthropy in Bedford and finishes in Luton, where he explores the dark arts of the hat-maker

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Audley End House, Essex, where items up for valuation include a large bronze cockerel that was buried during both world wars to escape being melted down for ammunition and a table that Napoleon may have signed his abdication on. One visitor to the show has a fascination with design diagrams inspired by an earlier episode - and brings in a collection of 650

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: An exploration of British landmarks, beginning with a look at the Tower of London - which has served as a castle, a jail and a place of execution

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan

programme.title: War Factories

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Oakham in Rutland, where he learns about a noble tradition dating back to the Middle Ages and decides to take part. Heading east to Stamford, Lincolnshire, he discovers why the town is such an attractive location for period dramas, while a ghoulish scene awaits in Peterborough when he visits a Victorian operating theatre where railwaymen were treated. Michael's last stop on his journey is Christ's College at Cambridge University, where he finds out about the student days of Charles Darwin

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment boss Duncan Powell hosts the final dinner party in Newport, Gwent, and hopes his Hawaiian-themed evening will impress his guests enough to win the prize. programme.desc: Niles is forced to pretend he is Mel's husband at a party, and Frasier hires a butler who spoils everyone in the household. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The radio shrink begins to date the attractive attorney who's defending him against Donny - who was jilted at the altar by Daphne - although he suspects that she is unfairly running up his bill

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Denver, Colorado, where he meets the stubborn owner of Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, who appears to be firmly stuck in the past. Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a couple from the West Midlands with three children to find a holiday home in Tenerife, tracking down five properties within their budget of £185,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: More of the most memorable moments from the past five years, looking back at some of Chester's more wily residents that have proven themselves to be expert escapologists. Meanwhile, three-month-old Andean bear cub Madidi escapes into the paddock next door

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: More of the most memorable moments from the past five years, including one of the zoo's most enduring love stories at Penguin Island. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.desc: Bubbly co-host Dr Mary joins Frasier to present a Christmas parade, but he is forced to take drastic action when she steals his limelight. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Arvada, Colorado, where a husband and wife have allowed their restaurant to fall into disrepair. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: A look at two very different manhunts involving the Devon and Cornwall police. The first case involves a car which flipped off the road while travelling at over 100mph and hit a tree. When officers arrive at the scene, they discover that the driver and passengers have fled the scene. A 17-strong team, with armed support, a dog unit and aerial assets, ventures into the surrounding woodland to discover where they have gone. Control also receives a call to be on the lookout for a missing man who is reported to be suicidal and whose whereabouts are uncertain

programme.title: Bring Back the Bush: Where Did Our Pubic Hair Go?
programme.desc: Author Chidera Eggerue explores people's relationship with pubic hair, investigating why so many women remove their pubic hair when even a few decades ago it wasn't seen as necessary. Along the way, she challenges herself and her peers to grow out their pubic hair and put on an exhibition in which they reveal their naked, natural bodies to the world

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the work of police in Northamptonshire, where, with resources stretched to breaking point, officers are struggling to meet the increased demand on their services. This edition looks at the impact of relationships on the force, with last year seeing were more than 13,000 domestic incidents in the county

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: With passenger numbers sky rocketing, airports and planes are increasingly becoming pressurised environments. Footage captured by people who were there highlights some of the most dramatic, extraordinary and unusual moments that UK passengers have experienced. From terrible behaviour in the terminal and tantrums on the tarmac to full-scale brawls on board, the programme hears from the people who were involved. Expert analysis from industry insiders and psychologists explains why people behave how they do on flights from hell

programme.title: Council House Britain
programme.desc: The work of frontline council workers in Southwark, south London, one of the biggest housing associations in the country. Cameras follow three-year-old Charlotte, who is one of the first children in the UK to take a pioneering new targeted drug that has saved her life, while toddler Artemis must undergo a stem cell transplant, first done over 50 years ago, as this is the best option available to her. programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates recipes for simple, affordable and delicious meals, from quick and tasty dinners to useful staples to add to regular repertoires

programme.desc: A father-of-three who is £30,000 in debt has to decide whether to accept a job offer that could help him clear his debts, or whether he would be better off staying on benefits. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Will & Grace

programme.title: My Wife and Kids
programme.desc: Claire wants to go on her first date with Tony, but Michael is reluctant to let her, so decides to spy on them in a bid to unmask the suitor as nothing more than a hormone-driven teenager

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.desc: Profile of a 67-year-old Somerset man who is hoping to get married for the ninth time, once his 27-year-old fiancee arrives from the Philippines. Ron believes that having Cristel around to care for him during in his old age will make him less of a burden on the state. Plus, a Lowestoft carer who looks after his wife hopes that moving into their first proper home together will bring them more happiness, and an out-of-work Liverpool man dreams of a life on the ocean waves

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.desc: Stories of people struggling on benefits. Mark from Cheltenham is a part-time drag queen who lost his Spanish bar after a licensing dispute. Now home and claiming sickness benefits for depression, he is keen to get well enough to work again. Plus, the stories of a widowed grandmother whose life has been tough since her drug-addicted grandson was sent to prison for attacking her, and a 63-year-old joiner who cannot work because of back trouble

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul Bohill and Phil Short must deal with a negligent tenant who fled upon their arrival, forcing the agents to set a trap in order to evict him. Brian O'Shaughnessy and Kevin Stokes chase a debt of £5,000 in Kent, before heading to Surrey to recover £11,000 owed by the owner a bespoke furniture business. Plus, Paul and Steve Pinner's patience is tested to the limit when they are sent to carry out an eviction in London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart McCracken and Elmore Victor head to Liverpool for what they expect to be a routine repossession, but instead face the most challenging eviction of their careers. Behind the front door, they find a critically ill two-year-old child with major heart problems, who is on constant life support and unable to breathe without a ventilator. Meanwhile, Brian O'Shaughnessy and Del Anglin chase £2,000 worth of debt owed by a business based in Kent, and recover £4,000 from a recycling business in Buckinghamshire. Plus, Stewart and Elmore encounter another family in crisis in Birmingham, where a retired couple who acted as guarantor for their daughter's tenancy are liable to pay over £3,000 after she defaulted

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Delroy and Kevin face a web of deceit in an Italian delicatessen in south London as they chase a huge debt amounting to £29,000. Meanwhile, Steve and Paul must evict a tenant in north London who has not paid her rent for over 18 months, before heading to Cambridgeshire to make one of their most emotional evictions to date. Plus, Brian and Delroy track down a pensioner in Surrey who owes £15,000 for a business he bought four years ago

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The enforcement agents try to mediate a dispute in a takeaway, and deal with rent evaders living in potentially deadly conditions. Plus, a businessman faces financial ruin

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers pursue a driver who flees the scene of a road accident in a decommissioned ambulance, eventually abandoning the vehicle on a railway line after crashing through level-crossing barriers. Elsewhere, Warren comes under attack from a gang of youths on a garage forecourt, Spenner nicks a runaway driver with the help of a wheelie bin, and Kev is faced with a flaming Mercedes in the middle of rush hour

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: In Huddersfield, officers are in pursuit of a van driver who smashed his van into a parked car before fleeing, while Bob and Dan pepper spray a suspect who is not keen on being handcuffed. Chris and Chris go after a stolen car whose driver gets too close to some railings, and the Operational Support Unit deal with a suspected heroin user carrying a number of bank cards and needles

Moving into their home will be Skye Walker and her three children. As a professional housekeeper, Skye works in houses not unlike the Sethard-Wrights'. programme.title: My Wife and Kids
programme.desc: Michael finds a grey hair and decides to take up boxing to get into shape. Meanwhile, Claire gets ready for her first date with Tony, but Junior mishears their conversation and jumps to the wrong conclusion. Comedy, starring Damon Wayans, Jennifer Nicole Freeman and Andrew McFarlane

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.desc: The stories of four people who have spent a total of almost 90 years on benefits. They include Greater Manchester couple Irene and Steve, who between them have been claiming for 70 years. Irene suffers with agoraphobia, and until recently husband Steve received a care allowance to look after her, but after a recent assessment their money has been cut by £6,000. Plus, a 49-year-old woman who once worked in the benefits office, only for her arthritis to leave her relying on the system, and a 57-year-old Navy veteran who suffers post-traumatic stress disorder after his experiences in the Gulf War

programme.title: On Benefits
programme.desc: The documentary series focuses on welfare recipients from the Merseyside area, including New Brighton man Jimmy, who struggles to find work as his religious beliefs prevent him from working on a Sunday, and Birkenhead single mum Nicola, who has become an expert bargain hunter to help stretch her benefits. Wallasey man Joe has a number of serious health conditions and hasn't had a shower at home in three years. However, after three years on the waiting list, a move to a specially adapted bungalow is finally on the cards

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The enforcement agents try to mediate a dispute in a takeaway, and deal with rent evaders living in potentially deadly conditions. Plus, a businessman faces financial ruin

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The debt collection agents grapple with emotional evictions and moral dilemmas during the festive period, including a young family in Crystal Palace who are made homeless when the landlord repossesses their home because he wants to redevelop the property. The eviction comes as a shock to the family as they are up to date with their rent, but have just one hour to gather their belongings. Plus, an acrimonious divorce in Chessington comes to a head when the estranged wife obtains a writ to reclaim the house, and an off-licence owner in Hampshire with unpaid rent of more than £5,000 battles to keep his business open just as everything shuts down for the holidays

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmore are met with lies and aggression when they track down nearly £3,000 owed by a garage mechanic, while Steve and Ben encounter a retired couple who are being forced to leave their home. Stuart and Vic revisit a young debtor who owe £750 for a laptop computer, and in Oxfordshire, Paul and Steve chase £30,000 owed by a world-famous Formula One racing team

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Iain head to Manchester, where a landlord is owed £8,000 in rent arrears and has already called for police back-up. As tempers rise, the pair must find a way to handle a situation that is spiralling dangerously out of control. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase £3,000 owed to a firm of letting agents, before tracking a debtor who was accidentally over-paid for a job. Plus, Stewart and Iain face an eviction that is set to test their patience

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A reckless driver gives Steve the slip in bad weather, but the suspect cannot escape the eye in the sky, while Ben has a run-in with an argumentative drink driver who fails three breath tests. Sophie and Chris surprise a disqualified driver in the team's unmarked car, and Warren is in high-speed pursuit of a stolen hatchback, concluding in a frantic foot race for rookie Kaine

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The police interception unit attempt to stop a dangerous driver, resulting in multiple cars and a helicopter being called into action. programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Close friends Janet Perrie and Fiona Hope moved to the South Ayrshire village of Dipple in search of an idyllic new life for themselves and their 22 dogs. Unfortunately for them, other residents were enraged by the noise, smell and mess and wasted no time in contacting the authorities. In a cul-de-sac in Leicestershire, the Pollard, Walne and Elliott families have been harassed by 68-year-old Leslie Collins for more than a decade after they complained about the noise from an illegal wood-waste business he was running from his home. He then began to target them with increasingly intimidating behaviour - including mooning, two-finger salutes, shouting abuse, cutting holes in hedges and spying on them with binoculars

programme.title: Rain Man (1988)

In a bid to get his hands on the money, he tracks down and abducts Raymond - but the ensuing cross-country journey proves to be a profound learning experience. Oscar-winning drama, starring Tom Cruise and Dustin Hoffman, with Valeria Golino, Gerald R Molen and Jack Murdock

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A doctor dies on board the USS Borealis during a heavy storm and when the ship is brought into Norfolk Naval Yard, Ducky's investigation reveals a telltale mark on the victim's carotid artery, suggesting he was injected with poison. Initial inquiries focus on a petty officer with a history of drug use who may have wanted the doctor to remain silent about his problems. American drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A jeweller's murder is linked to a prostitution business, and the subsequent trial puts McCoy's job on the line as interested parties try to call in old favours. Award-winning police drama, starring Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck ad executive becomes a murder suspect when his wife and son are killed during a night he claims to have spent drinking. Ellen Pompeo (Grey's Anatomy) guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An attorney is killed shortly after deciding to file a sexual harrassment claim against his employer, and when Briscoe and Curtis investigate further, they discover both the alleged harasser and his wife have excellent motives for murder

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A wealthy man is found dead in his bed, and the team receive an anonymous tip that he was murdered. Logan and Briscoe encounter difficulties at the scene when the widow and her attorney refuse to allow an autopsy. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy vows to find those responsible when a key witness to the killing of a drug dealer is shot dead. Suspicion falls on the accused's cousin, a local hoodlum with a number of charges to his name, and another associate who refuses to make a deal with the investigators. Sam Waterson, Elisabeth Rohm and Jerry Orbach star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A cross-dresser is suspected of killing and dismembering a cantankerous former boxer - but the team is unable to find vital body parts, making it impossible for the medical examiner to prove the case is murder. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to investigate the discovery of a US Marine's body at the foot of a cliff. Gibbs immediately suspects the man's ex-wife and his best friend are guilty of murder, and tries to turn them against each other. Meanwhile, Abby puts her technical skills to the test by trying to repair a camera that was broken during the victim's fall

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a petty officer is found lying in bed next to a barman - but he insists she is not the woman he brought home with him the previous night. The investigation leads Gibbs and the team to the victim's sister (R'n'B star Mya), who is a singer in a rival bartender's dance club. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed while competing in a swimsuit contest, raising fears she may have fallen victim to a jealous rival, or even a stalker. Gibbs' investigation leads to the discovery that the victim was taking yoga classes - and this provides him with the breakthrough he needs to solve the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An officer is found dead, and evidence initially suggests she committed suicide as a result of a nervous breakdown. However, a sceptical Ducky believes foul play was involved - and the team later discovers the victim was caught up in a love triangle. Kevin Quigley guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the case of a heart surgeon murdered during his son's bar mitzvah. Although they initially suspect someone with Mob connections, the detectives later discover another man's obsession puts him in the frame for the killing. Drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A doctor takes a paternity test that could prove his guilt in the murders of a mother and her baby. The procedure appears to prove he is not the father - but does implicate him in several child molestation cases. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler tries to rescue a youngster who has been kidnapped by a man with schizophrenia, but resorts to unorthodox tactics that jeopardise his own career. Novak intervenes to ensure his professional survival - and in return asks him to conduct an investigation at a secure psychiatric unit. Crime drama, guest starring Leland Orser

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A boy witnesses a fatal attack on his stepmother, but is too frightened to tell the police what happened. Benson and Stabler discover the dead woman was on the run from her abusive husband, taking her stepson with her, and before long the race is on to protect the lad from his father. Sandra Bernhard and Rob Estes guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The bomb squad is called out to deal with a suspicious suitcase found on a school bus - but it proves to contain the body of an eight-year-old beauty pageant princess. The detectives investigate her murder, and find themselves on the trail of a convicted sex offender with known dealings in pornographic websites

programme.title: Private Eyes
programme.desc: Shade and Angie are hired to find a violinist who went missing one year ago, and is now presumed dead. Meanwhile, Shade's dad begins to act strangely

programme.title: Private Eyes

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman is shot in her sleep, and further investigation leads the detectives to a boy whose drug-addicted mother has placed him in the care of her dealer. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The body of a superintendent is found in the basement of his building, leading Logan and Briscoe to suspect his death was the result of a break-in gone wrong. However, forensic evidence points the finger of suspicion at the victim's 17-year-old son, who Dr Olivet believes is an abusive sociopath. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A Holocaust survivor is murdered shortly before she is due to testify at the trial of a former SS Officer stationed at a concentration camp, forcing McCoy to consider who stood to gain the most from the old woman's death - the alleged war criminal, or a music producer promoting racist records

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A respected district attorney is found dead in Central Park - and as Briscoe and Green investigate, they discover he was actually living under an assumed identity, and had never graduated from law school. To complicate matters, further inquiries link his murder to that of a journalist 10 years previously. Guest starring Michael Bloomberg

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team try to find out who is responsible for sending a parcel containing a pair of blue human eyeballs to the home of a petty officer who works in Intelligence. However, the recipient of the organs commits suicide during the course of the inquiry, which leads the investigators to suspect one of his lecturers, who is working for the CIA in Panama

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed while competing in a swimsuit contest, raising fears she may have fallen victim to a jealous rival, or even a stalker. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is murdered, but it is not long before the prime suspect is also found dead. Tim and Abby discover a clue to both killings in an e-mail that suggests the victims were involved in a game with deadly consequences. Drama, starring Sean Murray and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine suspected of murdering a girl is killed in Iraq, and a search of his personal belongings reveals her body. Most members of the team believe they have found conclusive evidence of his guilt - but Gibbs suspects there is more to the situation than meets the eye

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An Alzheimer's patient claims to have been raped. Fin receives his sergeant orders. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay and Ice-T

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man is arrested for assaulting his alcoholic wife, who is pregnant - but changes the focus of the case by trying to have her charged with neglect for drinking while expecting a baby. As Benson investigates, he discovers a child had previously been exposed to the harmful consequences of her thoughtless actions

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a poster boy for a pressure group that tries to steer gay people towards heterosexuality. Suspicion falls on a cleric who was conducting a hate-mail campaign against the victim - but it transpires he has a watertight alibi. George Segal guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A quadruple homicide with sexual undertones marks the beginning of a killing spree that has the entire squad racing against the clock to prevent further murders. The investigation leads the detectives from one victim to another before the trail ends at a man with a painful secret. Crime drama, guest starring Frank Langella, Ian Somerhalder and Jason Ritter

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman is discovered in a bathroom next to the battered corpse of her boyfriend's brother, and immediately becomes the prime suspect in his murder. However, the discovery she is a pre-op transsexual leaves the team with new questions to consider. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Private Eyes

programme.title: Private Eyes

In Soho, police officers monitor the activity of the area's 100,000 nightly visitors from a CCTV control room in an underground bunker, while in Lambeth the air support unit is called in when an armed gang are seen rampaging through the streets

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Armed robbers break into the apartment of lawyer Roland Carson and make off in a helicopter with jewels worth more than $5million. The detectives investigate and, as people who have come into contact with the gems start to turn up dead, they suspect Carson could have staged the whole affair and killed his accomplices to cover his tracks. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A couple whose student son has been missing for a week fear the worst when they discover a badly decomposed body on the roof of a downtown warehouse, and the CSIs are called to investigate further. However, the case is turned on its head when the autopsy reveals the dead man's true identity, and an anonymous phone call leads the team on a wild chase through the city. Crime drama, guest starring Helen Slater

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A harmless tramp is attacked by an over-zealous vigilante and left for dead in a deserted alley, sending Briscoe and Logan in pursuit of his assailant. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Christopher Noth

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A college student is found dead, prompting the detective duo to search for a rapist on campus. However, a report from the medical examiner puts them on a different path, and they discover events may have gone down an even more sinister track

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A investigation into multiple homicides at a clothes shop leads to the arrest of a schizophrenic man, and his decision to represent himself in court provides McCoy with a formidable opponent

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A bomb is planted in a Wall Street car park, and a woman is apprehended in connection with the crime. However, Stone and Kincaid work to prove the culprit had been brainwashed by Daniel Hendricks, the charismatic leader of a sinister religious cult. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A bar-room brawl results in a baseball player being killed, leading Briscoe and Green to suspect a sporting grudge is behind the killing. However, a very different vendetta comes to light as the evidence points to a man recently exonerated after serving 20 years in prison for murder. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Jerry Orbach's final appearance in the crime drama. Briscoe's investigation into the killing of a delivery man uncovers the married victim's involvement in a website arranging romantic liaisons between consenting adults, and reveals the deceased's wife had recently purchased a gun

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An officer is found dead, and evidence initially suggests she committed suicide as a result of a nervous breakdown. However, a sceptical Ducky believes foul play was involved - and the team later discovers the victim was caught up in a love triangle. Kevin Quigley guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is murdered, but it is not long before the prime suspect is also found dead. Tim and Abby discover a clue to both killings in an e-mail that suggests the victims were involved in a game with deadly consequences. Drama, starring Sean Murray and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is infected with a deadly virus sent through the post by shadowy figures who demand the details of a long-closed case be made public in exchange for the antidote. McGee and Gibbs set out to identify the culprits and find a cure for the disease. Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's old enemy Ari Haswari returns, working on behalf of a secretive terrorist faction. Gibbs is distressed to learn he is the next target, but does not realise the killer has another more complex scheme in mind. Guest starring Rudolf Martin, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Private Eyes
programme.desc: Shade and Angie investigate a case of infidelity on a pirate-themed cruise ship, but get swept up in a far more villainous scheme

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A wealthy philanthropist who was accused of rape and kidnap is killed. Jacqueline Bisset guest stars alongside Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The discovery of a child's body sends Stabler and Cragen in search of a suspected paedophile, but important medical evidence emerges that implicates someone close to the child. Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A serial rapist represents himself in court and is released on bail, despite his first victim picking him out during an identity parade. Benson and Stabler refuse to give up the chase, but the criminal strikes again, and this time the result is murder. Fred Savage guest stars alongside Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A waitress is found raped and murdered outside a nightclub, with suspicion falling on her ex-boyfriend. Joe Morton (Law & Order) guest stars, alongside Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps two teachers from Milton Keynes find a retirement home on Wales' Lleyn Peninsula. After exploring the beautiful countryside, they examine six locations, including a converted chapel, a house overlooking the Menai Straits and the smallest property to appear in the series

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: Patients with a wide range of ailments are invited to attend the Food Hospital, where consultant surgeon Shaw Somers, dietitian Lucy Jones and GP Giovanni Miletto are among specialists aiming to discover if tailored nutritional programmes can help. Among those in need of assistance are a 24-year-old woman suffering from polycystic ovary syndrome, and a 44-year-old single father battling type 2 diabetes. Pixie McKenna also investigates the truth about superfoods and the programme features an experiment to see if chocolate can help combat high blood pressure

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the stories behind official royal residences, which act as family homes and places to conduct the business of monarchy, and charts the evolution of their architecture. She begins with Buckingham Palace and learns about its links to London sewers, its features including state rooms and a home for camels and elephants, plus how its chandeliers are cleaned

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Whistle Down the Wind (1961)
programme.desc: Three children become convinced that a bearded murderer taking refuge in the barn on their Lancashire farm is Jesus Christ, and do their utmost to protect him and conceal his presence from the outside world. Drama, based on the novel by Mary Hayley Bell, starring Alan Bates, Hayley Mills, Bernard Lee and Norman Bird

programme.title: Maria Marten: Or, Murder in the Red Barn (1935)
programme.desc: An evil squire lures a woman into marriage, but when she becomes pregnant, he decides to murder her, leaving a young gypsy who is in love with her to take the blame. Period drama, starring Tod Slaughter and Sophie Stewart

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a woman and her best friend search for properties along the North Yorkshire coast in Whitby, Filey and Scarborough, where their budget of £265,000 can buy them some impressive homes, which need to have enough room for a pet duck, goose and two chickens

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Supermarket Foods
programme.desc: Cherry Healey presents the first of two programmes in which she explores the unexpected positive health benefits of everyday foods, from the fat-burning properties of dairy to the brain-boosting berry that could get every student concentrating. She begins by tackling the battle of the bulge, with experts and members of the public helping test the common staples that might help everyone to lose weight

programme.title: Silk
programme.desc: Martha is asked to represent a man accused of raping his ex-girlfriend, and is forced to put aside her personal prejudices in an effort to prove her skills to the Silks panel. Meanwhile, novice barrister Niamh makes her first appearance in court, but a mix-up gives her an opportunity to claim a cheap victory. Legal drama, starring Maxine Peake and Natalie Dormer

programme.title: The Best of Men
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the birth of the Paralympics in 1948. Pioneering neurologist Dr Ludwig Guttmann arrives at Stoke Mandeville Hospital in Buckinghamshire and begins to transform the lives of his patients - Second World War servicemen with spinal cord injuries. He suggests using sport as a key part of their rehabilitation, and gets them involved in activities including wheelchair polo and basketball. Starring Eddie Marsan, Rob Brydon, George Mackay and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Loaded (1994)
programme.desc: Seven teenagers visit a deserted country house to shoot a film, but the gap between fiction and reality is soon being blurred. They decide to take acid as part of a strange ritual, which brings long-buried secrets and desires to light. Drama, starring Thandie Newton and Catherine McCormack

programme.title: The Conversations (2016)
programme.desc: A disillusioned dentist tries to become a stand-up comic, but finds he is unable to perform on stage due to his unrequited love for an American woman who is about to return to the States, never to be seen again. In desperation he seeks a way to have her permanently erased from his memory. Comedy drama, starring Haider Zafar and Daniella Down

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Drama following the lives of a London gangster and his family. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Originally broadcast in 1980

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: Brendan takes serious action after the tourists cause a disturbance at the hotel. The coach moves on to the next stage of the journey - a trip into the Alps that takes the passengers to Saint-Veran, the highest village in Europe

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a woman and her best friend search for properties along the North Yorkshire coast in Whitby, Filey and Scarborough, where their budget of £265,000 can buy them some impressive homes, which need to have enough room for a pet duck, goose and two chickens

programme.desc: The women of the village revolt against the men when Tjure refuses to help them with their household chores

programme.title: Vic the Viking

programme.title: Vic the Viking

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar and Vic go in search of a magic sword, unaware that an evil warlord plans to steal it from them once they have it

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Having overcome a bout of health problems, septuagenarians Ena and John Ryman are keen to live a little, and that means selling their Guildford home and searching for a property on the Jurassic Coast. With a budget of up to £500,000, Seetha Hallett shows them round an unusual upside down thatch in Lyme Regis and a restored Georgian apartment in Sidmouth

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Supermarket Foods
programme.desc: Cherry Healey presents the first of two programmes in which she explores the unexpected positive health benefits of everyday foods, from the fat-burning properties of dairy to the brain-boosting berry that could get every student concentrating. She begins by tackling the battle of the bulge, with experts and members of the public helping test the common staples that might help everyone to lose weight

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Three Steps in the Dark (1953)
programme.desc: A wealthy eccentric invites all the members of his family to his country house, so he can discuss the contents of his Will. However, when he is suddenly killed, everyone present is a suspect. Murder mystery, starring Greta Gynt and Hugh Sinclair

programme.title: The Slipper and the Rose (1976)
programme.desc: A girl is reduced to being the downtrodden servant of her cruel stepmother after her father's death. With a little help from her fairy godmother, she wins the heart of a handsome prince, who is desperately trying to avoid being forced into a political marriage by his parents. Musical version of Cinderella, starring Gemma Craven and Richard Chamberlain

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: A couple hope to swap the grime and grind of east London for a healthy life on the Pembrokeshire coast. Seetha Hallett shows them six properties in Tenby, Pembroke and the unique village of Saundersfoot, where rehearsals for the D-Day landings took place in 1943. But will their budget of £300,000 find a property big enough to accommodate the duo, some foster children and their grandmother?

Comedy, starring Martin Freeman, Susan Earl and Ken Morley

programme.title: Black Gunn (1972)
programme.desc: A member of a black militant group carries out a robbery on a crime syndicate that ends in several deaths. When he is later murdered by the gangsters, his nightclub owner brother swears revenge. Crime thriller, starring Jim Brown and Martin Landau

programme.title: 198 Grand (2019)
programme.desc: Three hapless criminals from South London get mixed up in an underworld double-cross, and find themselves in possession of a large sum of money that puts their lives in danger. Crime drama, starring Andy Frith and Dean Kilbey

programme.title: Deceitful Love (2016)
programme.desc: The lives of a wealthy British Indian family are turned upside down after some dark family secrets are revealed after the death of their father

programme.title: Queens
programme.desc: 16th-century Europe was a hotbed of tense international intrigue. Mary Stuart and Elizabeth I confront one another on matters religious, political and personal

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Part one of two. A tornado sweeps up the Drakkar and takes it to China - where a general arrests the Vikings, demanding to know how they made their ship fly

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a woman and her best friend search for properties along the North Yorkshire coast in Whitby, Filey and Scarborough, where their budget of £265,000 can buy them some impressive homes, which need to have enough room for a pet duck, goose and two chickens

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and the team are in Chester to learn about an experiment to find out whether standing up a few more hours each day could have major health benefits. Gabriel Weston witnesses a womb transplant, Saleyha Ahsan shows how to stop someone choking and Chris van Tulleken finds out if vitamin supplements are important to wellbeing

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks into the past of Edinburgh's Palace of Holyroodhouse, which is located close to the dormant volcano Arthur's Seat. Events in the royal residence, one of the smallest palaces in Britain, have determined the futures of England, France and Scotland, and it was the last place in which a royal prince challenged the right of an English king to sit on the throne. Fiona also examines objects including the Darnley jewel, which features numerous hidden messages, and the needlework of a doomed queen

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Lunch Hour (1961)
programme.desc: A junior executive turns his mind to romance, and sets off for a night of passion after reserving a room at a hotel - only for his torrid plans to go awry when his short-term relationship with a designer takes an unexpected turn. Comedy, starring Robert Stephens, Shirley Anne Field, Michael Robbins and Nigel Davenport

programme.title: Far from the Madding Crowd (1967)
programme.desc: A beautiful woman arouses the ardour of three rival suitors in 19th-century rural Dorset and, having flirted and toyed with their affections, she chooses to marry a caddish, gambling soldier - a decision that leads to a sequence of harrowing events. John Schlesinger's adaptation of Thomas Hardy's classic novel, with Julie Christie, Alan Bates, Terence Stamp and Peter Finch

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: British house-hunters Ric and Nita Halstead, who are seeking a dream home in Miami, receive expert advice before buying. Amanda Lamb shows them round an art deco house, a Mediterranean-style villa and a Florida ranch

programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely

programme.desc: Mike steadfastly resists a management attempt to make the staff wear identical uniforms, convinced it's a move designed to rob them of their individuality _ but a visit from Anne's surprisingly attractive mother soon diverts his attention to more personal matters. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman, Susan Earl and Ken Morley

programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist is joined by Stereophonics frontman Kelly Jones, who discusses some of his favourite songs, including Otis Redding's Cigarettes and Coffee and Who Killed Davey Moore? by Bob Dylan

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Drama following the lives of a London gangster and his family. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Originally broadcast in 1980

programme.title: Damage (1992)
programme.desc: An English politician at the height of his career risks everything he holds dear when he gives in to his impulses and recklessly embarks on a passionate relationship with his son's girlfriend. When news of the affair reaches Whitehall, the fallout looks set to end his political ambitions. Drama, starring Jeremy Irons, Juliette Binoche, Rupert Graves, and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Love, Honour and Obey (1999)
programme.desc: A humble courier manages to wheedle his way into the confidence of north London's toughest mobsters. Disappointed by the lack of action, he sets out to make things more interesting - but inadvertently sparks a bloody gangland conflict. Comedy thriller, starring Jude Law, Sadie Frost, Jonny Lee Miller, Ray Winstone, Rhys Ifans, Kathy Burke and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Cruel Passion (1977)
programme.desc: Two sisters are expelled from a nunnery and are plunged into a life of debauchery. While one embraces her new lifestyle, the other is desperate to cling to her old morality and sees a hope of salvation in a mysterious aristocrat who harbours a hidden dark side. Historical drama based on the novel Justine by the Marquis de Sade, starring Koo Stark and Martin Potter

programme.title: Ultimate Force
programme.desc: Armed Checkrein terrorists break into a pharmaceutical plant, taking hostages when the alarm is raised and the unit sealed off. Caroline volunteers to enter the premises and treat one of the employees after MI5 reveals the plant is working on a vaccine for a potent anthrax mutation. As time runs out, Red Troop is faced with a dilemma on which the lives of colleagues - and thousands of innocent civilians - depend. Ross Kemp and Alex Reid star

programme.desc: Short visual tours for locals and tourists, exploring Elephant & Castle and Croydon. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Liverpool v Cardiff City. Action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium, where the winner of the first major trophy of the 2011/12 season was decided. The Reds had defeated the likes of Stoke City, Chelsea and Manchester City to reach the final and went into the match as clear favourites in their first visit to the national stadium since it was rebuilt. However, the Bluebirds, who defeated Crystal Palace on penalties in the semi-finals, had been in good form in the Championship and will have fancied their chances of producing an upset
